capability_description: Providing cyber security leadership and guidance
name: ISM-0714
practice_description: A CISO is appointed to provide cyber security leadership and guidance for their organisation.

name: ISM-1478
practice_description: The CISO oversees their organisation's cyber security program and ensures their organisation's compliance with cyber security policy, standards, regulations and legislation.

name: ISM-1617
practice_description: The CISO regularly reviews and updates their organisation's cyber security program to ensure its relevance in addressing cyber threats and harnessing business and cyber security opportunities.

name: ISM-0724
practice_description: The CISO implements cyber security measurement metrics and key performance indicators for their organisation.

name: ISM-0725
practice_description: The CISO coordinates cyber security and business alignment through a cyber security steering committee or advisory board, comprising of key cyber security and business executives, which meets formally and on a regular basis.

name: ISM-0726
practice_description: The CISO coordinates security risk management activities between cyber security and business teams.

name: ISM-0718
practice_description: The CISO reports directly to their organisation's senior executive or Board on cyber security matters.

name: ISM-0733
practice_description: The CISO is fully aware of all cyber security incidents within their organisation.

name: ISM-1618
practice_description: The CISO oversees their organisation's response to cyber security incidents.

capability_description: Contributing to business continuity and disaster recovery planning
name: ISM-0734
practice_description: The CISO contributes to the development and maintenance of business continuity and disaster recovery plans for their organisation to ensure that business-critical services are supported appropriately in the event of a disaster.

capability_description: Developing a cyber security communications strategy
name: ISM-0720
practice_description: The CISO develops, implements and maintains a cyber security communications strategy for their organisation.

name: ISM-0731
practice_description: The CISO oversees cyber supply chain risk management activities for their organisation.

capability_description: Receiving and managing a dedicated cyber security budget
name: ISM-0732
practice_description: The CISO receives and manages a dedicated cyber security budget for their organisation.

name: ISM-0717
practice_description: The CISO oversees the management of cyber security personnel within their organisation.

name: ISM-0735
practice_description: The CISO oversees the development, implementation and maintenance of their organisation's cyber security awareness training program.

name: ISM-1071
practice_description: Each system has a designated system owner.

name: ISM-1525
practice_description: System owners register each system with its authorising officer.

name: ISM-1633
practice_description: System owners determine the type, value and security objectives for each system based on an assessment of the impact if it were to be compromised.

name: ISM-1634
practice_description: System owners select controls for each system and tailor them to achieve desired security objectives.

name: ISM-1635
practice_description: System owners implement controls for each system and its operating environment.

name: ISM-1636
practice_description: System owners ensure controls for each system and its operating environment are assessed to determine if they have been implemented correctly and are operating as intended.

name: ISM-0027
practice_description: System owners obtain authorisation to operate each system from its authorising officer based on the acceptance of the security risks associated with its operation.

name: ISM-1526
practice_description: System owners monitor each system, and associated cyber threats, security risks and controls, on an ongoing basis.

capability_description: Annual reporting of system security status
name: ISM-1587
practice_description: System owners report the security status of each system to its authorising officer at least annually.

name: ISM-0576
practice_description: An incident management policy, and associated incident response plan, is developed, implemented and maintained.

name: ISM-1784
practice_description: The incident management policy, including the associated incident response plan, is exercised at least annually.

name: ISM-0125
practice_description: A cyber security incident register is developed, implemented and maintained.

name: ISM-1803
practice_description: A cyber security incident register contains the following for each cyber security incident: • the date the cyber security incident occurred • the date the cyber security incident was discovered • a description of the cyber security incident • any actions taken in response to the cyber security incident • to whom the cyber security incident was reported.

name: ISM-1625
practice_description: A trusted insider program is developed, implemented and maintained.

name: ISM-1626
practice_description: Legal advice is sought regarding the development and implementation of a trusted insider program.

capability_description: Access to sufficient data sources and tools
name: ISM-0120
practice_description: Cyber security personnel have access to sufficient data sources and tools to ensure that systems can be monitored for key indicators of compromise.

name: ISM-0123
practice_description: Cyber security incidents are reported to an organisation's Chief Information Security Officer, or one of their delegates, as soon as possible after they occur or are discovered.

capability_description: Reporting cyber security incidents to the ACSC
name: ISM-0140
practice_description: Cyber security incidents are reported to the ACSC.

name: ISM-1819
practice_description: Following the identification of a cyber security incident, an organisation's incident response plan is enacted.

name: ISM-0133
practice_description: When a data spill occurs, data owners are advised and access to the data is restricted.

capability_description: Handling and containing malicious code infections
name: ISM-0917
practice_description: When malicious code is detected, the following steps are taken to handle the infection: • the infected systems are isolated • all previously connected media used in the period leading up to the infection are scanned for signs of infection and isolated if necessary • antivirus software is used to remove the infection from infected systems and media • if the infection cannot be reliably removed, systems are restored from a known good backup or rebuilt.

name: ISM-0137
practice_description: Legal advice is sought before allowing intrusion activity to continue on a system for the purpose of collecting further data or evidence.

name: ISM-1609
practice_description: System owners are consulted before allowing intrusion activity to continue on a system for the purpose of collecting further data or evidence.

name: ISM-1731
practice_description: Planning and coordination of intrusion remediation activities are conducted on a separate system to that which has been compromised.

name: ISM-1732
practice_description: To the extent possible, all intrusion remediation activities are conducted in a coordinated manner during the same planned outage.

name: ISM-1213
practice_description: Following intrusion remediation activities, full network traffic is captured for at least seven days and analysed to determine whether the adversary has been successfully removed from the system.

name: ISM-0138
practice_description: The integrity of evidence gathered during an investigation is maintained by investigators: • recording all of their actions • maintaining a proper chain of custody • following all instructions provided by relevant law enforcement agencies.

capability_description: Cyber supply chain risk management activities
name: ISM-1631
practice_description: Suppliers of applications, ICT equipment and services associated with systems are identified.

capability_description: Cyber supply chain risk management activities
name: ISM-1452
practice_description: A supply chain risk assessment is performed for suppliers of applications, ICT equipment and services in order to assess the impact to a system's security risk profile.

capability_description: Cyber supply chain risk management activities
name: ISM-1567
practice_description: Suppliers identified as high risk by a cyber supply chain risk assessment are not used.

capability_description: Cyber supply chain risk management activities
name: ISM-1568
practice_description: Applications, ICT equipment and services are chosen from suppliers that have demonstrated a commitment to the security of their products and services.

capability_description: Cyber supply chain risk management activities
name: ISM-1632
practice_description: Applications, ICT equipment and services are chosen from suppliers that have a strong track record of transparency and maintaining the security of their own systems and cyber supply chains.

capability_description: Cyber supply chain risk management activities
name: ISM-1569
practice_description: A shared responsibility model is created, documented and shared between suppliers and their customers in order to articulate the security responsibilities of each party.

name: ISM-1785
practice_description: A supplier relationship management policy is developed, implemented and maintained.

name: ISM-1786
practice_description: An approved supplier list is developed, implemented and maintained.

capability_description: Sourcing applications, ICT equipment and services
name: ISM-1787, ICT equipment and services
practice_description: Applications, ICT equipment and services are sourced from approved suppliers.

capability_description: Sourcing applications, ICT equipment and services
name: ISM-1788, ICT equipment and services
practice_description: Multiple potential suppliers are identified for sourcing critical applications, ICT equipment and services.

capability_description: Sourcing applications, ICT equipment and services
name: ISM-1789, ICT equipment and services
practice_description: Sufficient spares of critical ICT equipment are sourced and kept in reserve.

capability_description: Delivery of applications, ICT equipment and services
name: ISM-1790, ICT equipment and services
practice_description: Applications, ICT equipment and services are delivered in a manner that maintains their integrity.

capability_description: Delivery of applications, ICT equipment and services
name: ISM-1791, ICT equipment and services
practice_description: The integrity of applications, ICT equipment and services are assessed as part of acceptance of products and services.

capability_description: Delivery of applications, ICT equipment and services
name: ISM-1792, ICT equipment and services
practice_description: The authenticity of applications, ICT equipment and services are assessed as part of acceptance of products and services.

name: ISM-1736
practice_description: A managed service register is developed, implemented, maintained and verified on a regular basis.

name: ISM-1737
practice_description: A managed service register contains the following for each managed service: • managed service provider's name • managed service's name • purpose for using the managed service • sensitivity or classification of data involved • due date for the next security assessment of the managed service • contractual arrangements for the managed service • point of contact for users of the managed service • 24/7 contact details for the managed service provider.

name: ISM-1793
practice_description: Managed service providers and their managed services undergo a security assessment by an IRAP assessor at least every 24 months.

name: ISM-1637
practice_description: An outsourced cloud service register is developed, implemented, maintained and verified on a regular basis.

name: ISM-1638
practice_description: An outsourced cloud service register contains the following for each outsourced cloud service: • cloud service provider's name • cloud service's name • purpose for using the cloud service • sensitivity or classification of data involved • due date for the next security assessment of the cloud service • contractual arrangements for the cloud service • point of contact for users of the cloud service • 24/7 contact details for the cloud service provider.

name: ISM-1529
practice_description: Only community or private clouds are used for outsourced SECRET and TOP SECRET cloud services.

capability_description: Assessment of outsourced cloud service providers
name: ISM-1570
practice_description: Outsourced cloud service providers and their cloud services undergo a security assessment by an IRAP assessor at least every 24 months.

capability_description: Contractual security requirements with service providers
name: ISM-1395
practice_description: Service providers, including any subcontractors, provide an appropriate level of protection for any data entrusted to them or their services.

capability_description: Contractual security requirements with service providers
name: ISM-0072
practice_description: Security requirements associated with the confidentiality, integrity and availability of data are documented in contractual arrangements with service providers and reviewed on a regular and ongoing basis to ensure they remain fit for purpose.

capability_description: Contractual security requirements with service providers
name: ISM-1571
practice_description: The right to verify compliance with security requirements is documented in contractual arrangements with service providers.

capability_description: Contractual security requirements with service providers
name: ISM-1738
practice_description: The right to verify compliance with security requirements documented in contractual arrangements with service providers is exercised on a regular and ongoing basis.

capability_description: Contractual security requirements with service providers
name: ISM-1804
practice_description: Break clauses associated with failure to meet security requirements are documented in contractual arrangements with service providers.

capability_description: Contractual security requirements with service providers
name: ISM-0141
practice_description: The requirement for service providers to report cyber security incidents to a designated point of contact as soon as possible after they occur or are discovered is documented in contractual arrangements with service providers.

capability_description: Contractual security requirements with service providers
name: ISM-1794
practice_description: A minimum notification period of one month by service providers for significant changes to their own service provider arrangements is documented in contractual arrangements with service providers.

capability_description: Contractual security requirements with service providers
name: ISM-1451
practice_description: Types of data and its ownership is documented in contractual arrangements with service providers.

capability_description: Contractual security requirements with service providers
name: ISM-1572
practice_description: The regions or availability zones where data will be processed, stored and communicated is documented in contractual arrangements with service providers.

capability_description: Contractual security requirements with service providers
name: ISM-1573
practice_description: Access to all logs relating to an organisation's data and services is documented in contractual arrangements with service providers.

capability_description: Contractual security requirements with service providers
name: ISM-1574
practice_description: The storage of data in a portable manner that allows for backups, service migration and service decommissioning without any loss of data is documented in contractual arrangements with service providers.

capability_description: Contractual security requirements with service providers
name: ISM-1575
practice_description: A minimum notification period of one month for the cessation of any services by a service provider is documented in contractual arrangements with service providers.

capability_description: Access to systems and data by service providers
name: ISM-1073
practice_description: An organisation's systems and data are not accessed or administered by a service provider unless a contractual arrangement exists between the organisation and the service provider to do so.

capability_description: Access to systems and data by service providers
name: ISM-1576
practice_description: If an organisation's systems or data are accessed or administered by a service provider in an unauthorised manner, the organisation is immediately notified.

capability: Development and maintenance of security documentation
name: ISM-0039
practice_description: A cyber security strategy is developed, implemented and maintained.

capability: Development and maintenance of security documentation
name: ISM-0047
practice_description: Organisational-level security documentation is approved by the Chief Information Security Officer while system-specific security documentation is approved by the system's authorising officer.

capability: Development and maintenance of security documentation
name: ISM-1739
practice_description: A system's security architecture is approved prior to the development of the system.

capability: Development and maintenance of security documentation
name: ISM-0888
practice_description: Security documentation is reviewed at least annually and includes a ‘current as at [date]' or equivalent statement.

capability: Development and maintenance of security documentation
name: ISM-1602
practice_description: Security documentation, including notification of subsequent changes, is communicated to all stakeholders.

name: ISM-0041
practice_description: Systems have a system security plan that includes a description of the system and an annex that covers both applicable controls from this document and any additional controls that have been identified.

name: ISM-0043
practice_description: Systems have an incident response plan that covers the following: • guidelines on what constitutes a cyber security incident • the types of cyber security incidents likely to be encountered and the expected response to each type • how to report cyber security incidents, internally to an organisation and externally to relevant authorities • other parties which need to be informed in the event of a cyber security incident • the authority, or authorities, responsible for investigating and responding to cyber security incidents • the criteria by which an investigation of a cyber security incident would be requested from a law enforcement agency, the Australian Cyber Security Centre or other relevant authority • the steps necessary to ensure the integrity of evidence relating to a cyber security incident • system contingency measures or a reference to such details if they are located in a separate document.

name: ISM-1163
practice_description: Systems have a continuous monitoring plan that includes: • conducting vulnerability scans for systems at least monthly • conducting vulnerability assessments or penetration tests for systems at least annually • analysing identified security vulnerabilities to determine their potential impact • implementing mitigations based on risk, effectiveness and cost.

name: ISM-1563
practice_description: At the conclusion of a security assessment for a system, a security assessment report is produced by the assessor and covers: • the scope of the security assessment • the system's strengths and weaknesses • security risks associated with the operation of the system • the effectiveness of the implementation of controls • any recommended remediation actions.

name: ISM-1564
practice_description: At the conclusion of a security assessment for a system, a plan of action and milestones is produced by the system owner.

name: ISM-0810
practice_description: Systems are secured in facilities that meet the requirements for a security zone suitable for their sensitivity or classification.

capability_description: Physical access to servers, network devices and cryptographic equipment
name: ISM-1053, network devices and cryptographic equipment
practice_description: Servers, network devices and cryptographic equipment are secured in server rooms or communications rooms that meet the requirements for a security zone suitable for their sensitivity or classification.

capability_description: Physical access to servers, network devices and cryptographic equipment
name: ISM-1530, network devices and cryptographic equipment
practice_description: Servers, network devices and cryptographic equipment are secured in security containers or secure rooms suitable for their sensitivity or classification taking into account the combination of security zones they reside in.

capability_description: Physical access to servers, network devices and cryptographic equipment
name: ISM-0813, network devices and cryptographic equipment
practice_description: Server rooms, communications rooms, security containers and secure rooms are not left in unsecured states.

capability_description: Physical access to servers, network devices and cryptographic equipment
name: ISM-1074, network devices and cryptographic equipment
practice_description: Keys or equivalent access mechanisms to server rooms, communications rooms, security containers and secure rooms are appropriately controlled.

capability_description: Physical access to network devices in public areas
name: ISM-1296
practice_description: Physical security is implemented to protect network devices in public areas from physical damage or unauthorised access.

capability_description: Bringing Radio Frequency and infrared devices into facilities
name: ISM-1543
practice_description: An authorised RF and IR device register for SECRET and TOP SECRET areas is developed, implemented, maintained and verified on a regular basis.

capability_description: Bringing Radio Frequency and infrared devices into facilities
name: ISM-0225
practice_description: Unauthorised RF and IR devices are not brought into SECRET and TOP SECRET areas.

capability_description: Bringing Radio Frequency and infrared devices into facilities
name: ISM-0829
practice_description: Security measures are used to detect and respond to unauthorised RF devices in SECRET and TOP SECRET areas.

name: ISM-0164
practice_description: Unauthorised people are prevented from observing systems, in particular workstation displays and keyboards, within facilities.

name: ISM-0161
practice_description: ICT equipment and media are secured when not in use.

name: ISM-0252
practice_description: Cyber security awareness training is undertaken annually by all personnel and covers: • the purpose of the cyber security awareness training • security appointments and contacts • authorised use of systems and their resources • protection of systems and their resources • reporting of cyber security incidents and suspected compromises of systems and their resources.

name: ISM-1565
practice_description: Tailored privileged user training is undertaken annually by all privileged users.

capability_description: Managing and reporting suspicious changes to banking details or payment requests
name: ISM-1740
practice_description: Personnel dealing with banking details and payment requests are advised of what business email compromise is, how to manage such situations and how to report it.

capability_description: Reporting suspicious contact via online services
name: ISM-0817
practice_description: Personnel are advised of what suspicious contact via online services is and how to report it.

capability_description: Posting work information to online services
name: ISM-0820
practice_description: Personnel are advised to not post work information to unauthorised online services and to report cases where such information is posted.

capability_description: Posting work information to online services
name: ISM-1146
practice_description: Personnel are advised to maintain separate work and personal accounts for online services.

capability_description: Posting personal information to online services
name: ISM-0821
practice_description: Personnel are advised of security risks associated with posting personal information to online services and are encouraged to use any available privacy settings to restrict who can view such information.

capability_description: Sending and receiving files via online services
name: ISM-0824
practice_description: Personnel are advised not to send or receive files via unauthorised online services.

capability: Access to systems and their resources
name: ISM-0432
practice_description: Access requirements for a system and its resources are documented in its system security plan.

capability: Access to systems and their resources
name: ISM-0434
practice_description: Personnel undergo appropriate employment screening and, where necessary, hold an appropriate security clearance before being granted access to a system and its resources.

capability: Access to systems and their resources
name: ISM-0435
practice_description: Personnel receive any necessary briefings before being granted access to a system and its resources.

capability: Access to systems and their resources
name: ISM-0414
practice_description: Personnel granted access to a system and its resources are uniquely identifiable.

capability: Access to systems and their resources
name: ISM-0415
practice_description: The use of shared user accounts is strictly controlled, and personnel using such accounts are uniquely identifiable.

capability: Access to systems and their resources
name: ISM-1583
practice_description: Personnel who are contractors are identified as such.

capability: Access to systems and their resources
name: ISM-0420
practice_description: Where a system processes, stores or communicates AUSTEO, AGAO or REL data, personnel who are foreign nationals are identified as such, including by their specific nationality.

capability: Access to systems and their resources
name: ISM-0405
practice_description: Requests for unprivileged access to systems, applications and data repositories are validated when first requested.

capability: Access to systems and their resources
name: ISM-1566
practice_description: Use of unprivileged access is logged.

capability: Access to systems and their resources
name: ISM-1714
practice_description: Unprivileged access event logs are stored centrally.

capability: Access to systems and their resources
capability_description: Unprivileged access to systems by foreign nationals
name: ISM-0409
practice_description: Foreign nationals, including seconded foreign nationals, do not have access to systems that process, store or communicate AUSTEO or REL data unless effective controls are in place to ensure such data is not accessible to them.

capability: Access to systems and their resources
capability_description: Unprivileged access to systems by foreign nationals
name: ISM-0411
practice_description: Foreign nationals, excluding seconded foreign nationals, do not have access to systems that process, store or communicate AGAO data unless effective controls are in place to ensure such data is not accessible to them.

capability: Access to systems and their resources
name: ISM-1507
practice_description: Requests for privileged access to systems and applications are validated when first requested.
expanded_guidance: ## Guidance on restricting administrative privileges can be found here: [Restricting Administrative Privileges](https://www.cyber.gov.au/resources-business-and-government/maintaining-devices-and-systems/system-hardening-and-administration/system-administration/restricting-administrative-privileges)

capability: Access to systems and their resources
name: ISM-1733
practice_description: Requests for privileged access to data repositories are validated when first requested.
expanded_guidance: ## Guidance on restricting administrative privileges can be found here: [Restricting Administrative Privileges](https://www.cyber.gov.au/resources-business-and-government/maintaining-devices-and-systems/system-hardening-and-administration/system-administration/restricting-administrative-privileges)

capability: Access to systems and their resources
name: ISM-1508
practice_description: Privileged access to systems and applications is limited to only what is required for users and services to undertake their duties.
expanded_guidance: ## Guidance on restricting administrative privileges can be found here: [Restricting Administrative Privileges](https://www.cyber.gov.au/resources-business-and-government/maintaining-devices-and-systems/system-hardening-and-administration/system-administration/restricting-administrative-privileges)

capability: Access to systems and their resources
name: ISM-1175
practice_description: Privileged user accounts are prevented from accessing the internet, email and web services.
expanded_guidance: ## Guidance on restricting administrative privileges can be found here: [Restricting Administrative Privileges](https://www.cyber.gov.au/resources-business-and-government/maintaining-devices-and-systems/system-hardening-and-administration/system-administration/restricting-administrative-privileges)

capability: Access to systems and their resources
name: ISM-1653
practice_description: Privileged service accounts are prevented from accessing the internet, email and web services.
expanded_guidance: ## Guidance on restricting administrative privileges can be found here: [Restricting Administrative Privileges](https://www.cyber.gov.au/resources-business-and-government/maintaining-devices-and-systems/system-hardening-and-administration/system-administration/restricting-administrative-privileges)

capability: Access to systems and their resources
name: ISM-1649
practice_description: Just-in-time administration is used for administering systems and applications.
expanded_guidance: ## Guidance on restricting administrative privileges can be found here: [Restricting Administrative Privileges](https://www.cyber.gov.au/resources-business-and-government/maintaining-devices-and-systems/system-hardening-and-administration/system-administration/restricting-administrative-privileges)

capability: Access to systems and their resources
name: ISM-0445
practice_description: Privileged users are assigned a dedicated privileged account to be used solely for tasks requiring privileged access.
expanded_guidance: ## Guidance on restricting administrative privileges can be found here: [Restricting Administrative Privileges](https://www.cyber.gov.au/resources-business-and-government/maintaining-devices-and-systems/system-hardening-and-administration/system-administration/restricting-administrative-privileges)

capability: Access to systems and their resources
name: ISM-1263
practice_description: Unique privileged accounts are used for administering individual server applications.
expanded_guidance: ## Guidance on restricting administrative privileges can be found here: [Restricting Administrative Privileges](https://www.cyber.gov.au/resources-business-and-government/maintaining-devices-and-systems/system-hardening-and-administration/system-administration/restricting-administrative-privileges)

capability: Access to systems and their resources
name: ISM-1509
practice_description: Privileged access events are logged.
expanded_guidance: ## Guidance on restricting administrative privileges can be found here: [Restricting Administrative Privileges](https://www.cyber.gov.au/resources-business-and-government/maintaining-devices-and-systems/system-hardening-and-administration/system-administration/restricting-administrative-privileges)

capability: Access to systems and their resources
name: ISM-1651
practice_description: Privileged access event logs are stored centrally.
expanded_guidance: ## Guidance on restricting administrative privileges can be found here: [Restricting Administrative Privileges](https://www.cyber.gov.au/resources-business-and-government/maintaining-devices-and-systems/system-hardening-and-administration/system-administration/restricting-administrative-privileges)

capability: Access to systems and their resources
name: ISM-1650
practice_description: Privileged account and group management events are logged.
expanded_guidance: ## Guidance on restricting administrative privileges can be found here: [Restricting Administrative Privileges](https://www.cyber.gov.au/resources-business-and-government/maintaining-devices-and-systems/system-hardening-and-administration/system-administration/restricting-administrative-privileges)

capability: Access to systems and their resources
name: ISM-1652
practice_description: Privileged account and group management event logs are stored centrally.
expanded_guidance: ## Guidance on restricting administrative privileges can be found here: [Restricting Administrative Privileges](https://www.cyber.gov.au/resources-business-and-government/maintaining-devices-and-systems/system-hardening-and-administration/system-administration/restricting-administrative-privileges)

capability: Access to systems and their resources
capability_description: Privileged access to systems by foreign nationals
name: ISM-0446
practice_description: Foreign nationals, including seconded foreign nationals, do not have privileged access to systems that process, store or communicate AUSTEO or REL data.
expanded_guidance: ## Guidance on restricting administrative privileges can be found here: [Restricting Administrative Privileges](https://www.cyber.gov.au/resources-business-and-government/maintaining-devices-and-systems/system-hardening-and-administration/system-administration/restricting-administrative-privileges)

capability: Access to systems and their resources
capability_description: Privileged access to systems by foreign nationals
name: ISM-0447
practice_description: Foreign nationals, excluding seconded foreign nationals, do not have privileged access to systems that process, store or communicate AGAO data.
expanded_guidance: ## Guidance on restricting administrative privileges can be found here: [Restricting Administrative Privileges](https://www.cyber.gov.au/resources-business-and-government/maintaining-devices-and-systems/system-hardening-and-administration/system-administration/restricting-administrative-privileges)

capability: Access to systems and their resources
name: ISM-0430
practice_description: Access to systems, applications and data repositories is removed or suspended on the same day personnel no longer have a legitimate requirement for access.

capability: Access to systems and their resources
name: ISM-1591
practice_description: Access to systems, applications and data repositories is removed or suspended as soon as practicable when personnel are detected undertaking malicious activities.

capability: Access to systems and their resources
name: ISM-1404
practice_description: Unprivileged access to systems and applications is automatically disabled after 45 days of inactivity.

capability: Access to systems and their resources
name: ISM-1648
practice_description: Privileged access to systems and applications is automatically disabled after 45 days of inactivity.

capability: Access to systems and their resources
name: ISM-1716
practice_description: Access to data repositories is automatically disabled after 45 days of inactivity.

capability: Access to systems and their resources
name: ISM-1647
practice_description: Privileged access to systems and applications is automatically disabled after 12 months unless revalidated.

capability: Access to systems and their resources
name: ISM-1734
practice_description: Privileged access to data repositories is automatically disabled after 12 months unless revalidated.

capability: Access to systems and their resources
capability_description: Recording authorisation for personnel to access systems
name: ISM-0407
practice_description: A secure record is maintained for the life of each system covering: • all personnel authorised to access the system, and their user identification • who provided authorisation for access • when access was granted • the level of access that was granted • when access, and the level of access, was last reviewed • when the level of access was changed, and to what extent (if applicable) • when access was withdrawn (if applicable).

capability: Access to systems and their resources
name: ISM-0441
practice_description: When personnel are granted temporary access to a system, effective controls are put in place to restrict their access to only data required for them to undertake their duties.

capability: Access to systems and their resources
name: ISM-0443
practice_description: Temporary access is not granted to systems that process, store or communicate caveated or sensitive compartmented information.

capability: Access to systems and their resources
name: ISM-1610
practice_description: A method of emergency access to systems is documented and tested at least once when initially implemented and each time fundamental information technology infrastructure changes occur.

capability: Access to systems and their resources
name: ISM-1611
practice_description: Break glass accounts are only used when normal authentication processes cannot be used.

capability: Access to systems and their resources
name: ISM-1612
practice_description: Break glass accounts are only used for specific authorised activities.

capability: Access to systems and their resources
name: ISM-1614
practice_description: Break glass account credentials are changed by the account custodian after they are accessed by any other party.

capability: Access to systems and their resources
name: ISM-1615
practice_description: Break glass accounts are tested after credentials are changed.

capability: Access to systems and their resources
name: ISM-1613
practice_description: Use of break glass accounts is logged.

capability: Access to systems and their resources
name: ISM-1715
practice_description: Break glass event logs are stored centrally.

capability: Access to systems and their resources
name: ISM-0078
practice_description: Systems processing, storing or communicating AUSTEO or AGAO data remain at all times under the control of an Australian national working for or on behalf of the Australian Government.

capability: Access to systems and their resources
name: ISM-0854
practice_description: AUSTEO and AGAO data can only be accessed from systems under the sole control of the Australian Government that are located within facilities authorised by the Australian Government.

name: ISM-0181
practice_description: Cabling infrastructure is installed in accordance with relevant Australian Standards, as directed by the Australian Communications and Media Authority.

name: ISM-1111
practice_description: Fibre-optic cables are used for cabling infrastructure instead of copper cables.

name: ISM-0211
practice_description: A cable register is developed, implemented, maintained and verified on a regular basis.

name: ISM-0208
practice_description: A cable register contains the following for each cable: • cable identifier • cable colour • sensitivity/classification • source • destination • location • seal numbers (if applicable).

name: ISM-1645
practice_description: Floor plan diagrams are developed, implemented, maintained and verified on a regular basis.

name: ISM-1646
practice_description: Floor plan diagrams contain the following: • cable paths (including ingress and egress points between floors) • cable reticulation system and conduit paths • floor concentration boxes • wall outlet boxes • network cabinets.

name: ISM-0206
practice_description: Cable labelling processes, and supporting cable labelling procedures, are developed, implemented and maintained.

name: ISM-1096
practice_description: Cables are labelled at each end with sufficient source and destination details to enable the physical identification and inspection of the cable.

name: ISM-1639
practice_description: Building management cables are labelled with their purpose in black writing on a yellow background, with a minimum size of 2.5 cm x 1 cm, and attached at five-metre intervals.

capability_description: Labelling cables for foreign systems in Australian facilities
name: ISM-1640
practice_description: Cables for foreign systems installed in Australian facilities are labelled at inspection points.

name: ISM-1820
practice_description: Cables for individual systems use a consistent colour.

name: ISM-0926
practice_description: OFFICIAL and PROTECTED cables are coloured neither salmon pink nor red.

name: ISM-1718
practice_description: SECRET cables are coloured salmon pink.

name: ISM-1719
practice_description: TOP SECRET cables are coloured red.

name: ISM-1216
practice_description: SECRET and TOP SECRET cables with non-conformant cable colouring are both banded with the appropriate colour and labelled at inspection points.

name: ISM-1112
practice_description: Cables are inspectable at a minimum of five-metre intervals.

name: ISM-1119
practice_description: Cables in TOP SECRET areas are fully inspectable for their entire length.

name: ISM-0187
practice_description: SECRET cables, when bundled together or run in conduit, are run exclusively in their own individual cable bundle or conduit.

name: ISM-1821
practice_description: TOP SECRET cables, when bundled together or run in conduit, are run exclusively in their own individual cable bundle or conduit.

name: ISM-1114
practice_description: Cable bundles or conduits sharing a common cable reticulation system have a dividing partition or visible gap between each cable bundle and conduit.

name: ISM-1130
practice_description: In shared facilities, cables are run in an enclosed cable reticulation system.

capability_description: Covers for enclosed cable reticulation systems
name: ISM-1164
practice_description: In shared facilities, conduits or the front covers of ducts, cable trays in floors and ceilings, and associated fittings are clear plastic.

capability_description: Sealing cable reticulation systems and conduits
name: ISM-0195
practice_description: In shared facilities, uniquely identifiable SCEC-approved tamper-evident seals are used to seal all removable covers on TOP SECRET cable reticulation systems.

capability_description: Sealing cable reticulation systems and conduits
name: ISM-0194
practice_description: In shared facilities, a visible smear of conduit glue is used to seal all plastic conduit joints and TOP SECRET conduits connected by threaded lock nuts.

name: ISM-0201
practice_description: Labels for TOP SECRET conduits are a minimum size of 2.5 cm x 1 cm, attached at five-metre intervals and marked as ‘TS RUN'.

name: ISM-1115
practice_description: Cables from cable trays to wall outlet boxes are run in flexible or plastic conduit.

name: ISM-1133
practice_description: In shared facilities, TOP SECRET cables are not run in party walls.

name: ISM-1122
practice_description: Where wall penetrations exit a TOP SECRET area into a lower classified area, TOP SECRET cables are encased in conduit with all gaps between the TOP SECRET conduit and the wall filled with an appropriate sealing compound.

name: ISM-1105
practice_description: SECRET and TOP SECRET wall outlet boxes contain exclusively SECRET or TOP SECRET cables.

name: ISM-1095
practice_description: Wall outlet boxes denote the systems, cable identifiers and wall outlet box identifier.

name: ISM-1822
practice_description: Wall outlet boxes for individual systems use a consistent colour.

name: ISM-1107
practice_description: OFFICIAL and PROTECTED wall outlet boxes are coloured neither salmon pink nor red.

name: ISM-1720
practice_description: SECRET wall outlet boxes are coloured salmon pink.

name: ISM-1721
practice_description: TOP SECRET wall outlet boxes are coloured red.

name: ISM-1109
practice_description: Wall outlet box covers are clear plastic.

name: ISM-0218
practice_description: If TOP SECRET fibre-optic fly leads exceeding five metres in length are used to connect wall outlet boxes to ICT equipment, they are run in a protective and easily inspected pathway that is clearly labelled at the ICT equipment end with the wall outlet box's identifier.

capability_description: Connecting cable reticulation systems to cabinets
name: ISM-1102
practice_description: Cable reticulation systems leading into cabinets are terminated as close as possible to the cabinet.

capability_description: Connecting cable reticulation systems to cabinets
name: ISM-1101
practice_description: In TOP SECRET areas, cable reticulation systems leading into cabinets in server rooms or communications rooms are terminated as close as possible to the cabinet.

capability_description: Connecting cable reticulation systems to cabinets
name: ISM-1103
practice_description: In TOP SECRET areas, cable reticulation systems leading into cabinets not in server rooms or communications rooms are terminated at the boundary of the cabinet.

name: ISM-1098
practice_description: SECRET cables are terminated in an individual cabinet; or for small systems, a cabinet with a division plate between any SECRET cables and non-SECRET cables.

name: ISM-1100
practice_description: TOP SECRET cables are terminated in an individual TOP SECRET cabinet.

name: ISM-0213
practice_description: SECRET and TOP SECRET cables are terminated on their own individual patch panels.

capability_description: Physical separation of cabinets and patch panels
name: ISM-0216
practice_description: TOP SECRET patch panels are installed in individual TOP SECRET cabinets.

capability_description: Physical separation of cabinets and patch panels
name: ISM-0217
practice_description: Where spatial constraints demand non-TOP SECRET patch panels be installed in the same cabinet as a TOP SECRET patch panel: • a physical barrier in the cabinet is provided to separate patch panels • only personnel holding a Positive Vetting security clearance have access to the cabinet • approval from the TOP SECRET system's authorising officer is obtained prior to installation.

capability_description: Physical separation of cabinets and patch panels
name: ISM-1116
practice_description: A visible gap exists between TOP SECRET cabinets and non-TOP SECRET cabinets.

name: ISM-0198
practice_description: When penetrating a TOP SECRET audio secure room, the Australian Security Intelligence Organisation is consulted and all directions provided are complied with.

name: ISM-1123
practice_description: A power distribution board with a feed from an Uninterruptible Power Supply is used to power all TOP SECRET ICT equipment.

capability_description: Emanation security threat assessments in Australia
name: ISM-0248
practice_description: System owners deploying OFFICIAL or PROTECTED systems with Radio Frequency transmitters that will be co-located with SECRET or TOP SECRET systems contact the ACSC for an emanation security threat assessment and implement any additional installation criteria derived from the threat assessment.

capability_description: Emanation security threat assessments in Australia
name: ISM-0247
practice_description: System owners deploying SECRET or TOP SECRET systems with Radio Frequency transmitters inside or co-located with their facility contact the ACSC for an emanation security threat assessment and implement any additional installation criteria derived from the threat assessment.

capability_description: Emanation security threat assessments in Australia
name: ISM-1137
practice_description: System owners deploying SECRET or TOP SECRET systems in shared facilities contact the ACSC for an emanation security threat assessment and implement any additional installation criteria derived from the threat assessment.

capability_description: Emanation security threat assessments outside Australia
name: ISM-0249
practice_description: System owners deploying systems or military platforms overseas contact the ACSC for an emanation security threat assessment and implement any additional installation criteria derived from the threat assessment.

capability_description: Early consideration of emanation security threats
name: ISM-0246
practice_description: An emanation security threat assessment is sought as early as possible in a system's life cycle as implementing emanation security can have significant cost implications.

name: ISM-0250
practice_description: ICT equipment meets industry and government standards relating to electromagnetic interference/electromagnetic compatibility.

name: ISM-1078
practice_description: A telephone system usage policy is developed, implemented and maintained.

name: ISM-0229
practice_description: Personnel are advised of the permitted sensitivity or classification of information that can be discussed over both internal and external telephone systems.

name: ISM-0230
practice_description: Personnel are advised of security risks posed by non-secure telephone systems in areas where sensitive or classified conversations can occur.

name: ISM-0231
practice_description: When using cryptographic equipment to permit different levels of conversation for different kinds of connections, telephone systems give a visual indication of what kind of connection has been made.

name: ISM-0232
practice_description: Telephone systems used for sensitive or classified conversations encrypt all traffic that passes over external systems.

name: ISM-0233
practice_description: Cordless telephone handsets and headsets are not used for sensitive or classified conversations unless all communications are encrypted.

name: ISM-0235
practice_description: Speakerphones are not used on telephone systems in TOP SECRET areas unless the telephone system is located in an audio secure room, the room is audio secure during conversations and only personnel involved in conversations are present in the room.

name: ISM-0236
practice_description: Off-hook audio protection features are used on telephone systems in areas where background conversations may exceed the sensitivity or classification that the telephone system is authorised for communicating.

name: ISM-0931
practice_description: In SECRET and TOP SECRET areas, push-to-talk handsets or push-to-talk headsets are used to meet any off-hook audio protection requirements.

capability: Video conferencing and Internet Protocol telephony
capability_description: Video conferencing and Internet Protocol telephony infrastructure hardening
name: ISM-1562
practice_description: Video conferencing and IP telephony infrastructure is hardened.

capability: Video conferencing and Internet Protocol telephony
capability_description: Video-aware and voice-aware firewalls and proxies
name: ISM-0546
practice_description: When video conferencing or IP telephony traffic passes through a gateway containing a firewall or proxy, a video-aware or voice-aware firewall or proxy is used.

capability: Video conferencing and Internet Protocol telephony
capability_description: Protecting video conferencing and Internet Protocol telephony traffic
name: ISM-0548
practice_description: Video conferencing and IP telephony calls are established using a secure session initiation protocol.

capability: Video conferencing and Internet Protocol telephony
capability_description: Protecting video conferencing and Internet Protocol telephony traffic
name: ISM-0547
practice_description: Video conferencing and IP telephony calls are conducted using a secure real-time transport protocol.

capability: Video conferencing and Internet Protocol telephony
capability_description: Video conferencing unit and Internet Protocol phone authentication
name: ISM-0554
practice_description: An encrypted and non-replayable two-way authentication scheme is used for call authentication and authorisation.

capability: Video conferencing and Internet Protocol telephony
capability_description: Video conferencing unit and Internet Protocol phone authentication
name: ISM-0553
practice_description: Authentication and authorisation is used for all actions on a video conferencing network, including call setup and changing settings.

capability: Video conferencing and Internet Protocol telephony
capability_description: Video conferencing unit and Internet Protocol phone authentication
name: ISM-0555
practice_description: Authentication and authorisation is used for all actions on an IP telephony network, including registering a new IP phone, changing phone users, changing settings and accessing voicemail.

capability: Video conferencing and Internet Protocol telephony
capability_description: Video conferencing unit and Internet Protocol phone authentication
name: ISM-0551
practice_description: IP telephony is configured such that: • IP phones authenticate themselves to the call controller upon registration • auto-registration is disabled and only authorised devices are allowed to access the network • unauthorised devices are blocked by default • all unused and prohibited functionality is disabled.

capability: Video conferencing and Internet Protocol telephony
capability_description: Video conferencing unit and Internet Protocol phone authentication
name: ISM-1014
practice_description: Individual logins are implemented for IP phones used for SECRET or TOP SECRET conversations.

capability: Video conferencing and Internet Protocol telephony
name: ISM-0549
practice_description: Video conferencing and IP telephony traffic is separated physically or logically from other data traffic.

capability: Video conferencing and Internet Protocol telephony
name: ISM-0556
practice_description: Workstations are not connected to video conferencing units or IP phones unless the workstation or the device uses Virtual Local Area Networks or similar mechanisms to maintain separation between video conferencing, IP telephony and other data traffic.

capability: Video conferencing and Internet Protocol telephony
capability_description: Internet Protocol phones in public areas
name: ISM-0558
practice_description: IP phones used in public areas do not have the ability to access data networks, voicemail and directory services.

capability: Video conferencing and Internet Protocol telephony
name: ISM-0559
practice_description: Microphones (including headsets and USB handsets) and webcams are not used with non-SECRET workstations in SECRET areas.

capability: Video conferencing and Internet Protocol telephony
name: ISM-1450
practice_description: Microphones (including headsets and USB handsets) and webcams are not used with non-TOP SECRET workstations in TOP SECRET areas.

capability: Video conferencing and Internet Protocol telephony
name: ISM-1019
practice_description: A denial of service response plan for video conferencing and IP telephony services is developed, implemented and maintained.

capability: Video conferencing and Internet Protocol telephony
name: ISM-1805
practice_description: A denial of service response plan for video conferencing and IP telephony services contains the following: • how to identify signs of a denial-of-service attack • how to identify the source of a denial-of-service attack • how capabilities can be maintained during a denial-of-service attack • what actions can be taken to respond to a denial-of-service attack.

capability_description: Fax machine and multifunction device usage policy
name: ISM-0588
practice_description: A fax machine and MFD usage policy is developed, implemented and maintained.

name: ISM-1092
practice_description: Separate fax machines or MFDs are used for sending sensitive or classified fax messages and all other fax messages.

name: ISM-0241
practice_description: When sending fax messages, the fax message is encrypted to an appropriate level to be communicated over unsecured telecommunications infrastructure.

name: ISM-1075
practice_description: The sender of a fax message makes arrangements for the receiver to collect the fax message as soon as possible after it is sent and for the receiver to notify the sender if the fax message does not arrive in an agreed amount of time.

name: ISM-0590
practice_description: Controls for MFDs connected to networks are of a similar strength to those for other devices on networks.

capability_description: Connecting multifunction devices to both networks and digital telephone systems
name: ISM-0245
practice_description: A direct connection from an MFD to a digital telephone system is not enabled unless the digital telephone system is authorised to operate at the same sensitivity or classification as the network to which the MFD is connected.

name: ISM-0589
practice_description: MFDs connected to networks are not used to copy documents above the sensitivity or classification of connected networks.

capability_description: Observing fax machine and multifunction device use
name: ISM-1036
practice_description: Fax machines and MFDs are located in areas where their use can be observed.

name: ISM-1533
practice_description: A mobile device management policy is developed, implemented and maintained.

name: ISM-1195
practice_description: A Mobile Device Management solution is used to ensure mobile device management policy is applied to all mobile devices.

name: ISM-0687
practice_description: Mobile devices do not process, store or communicate SECRET or TOP SECRET data until approved for use by ASD.

name: ISM-1297
practice_description: Legal advice is sought prior to allowing privately-owned mobile devices to access systems or data.

name: ISM-1400
practice_description: Personnel accessing OFFICIAL and PROTECTED systems or data using a privately-owned mobile device use an ASD-approved platform, a security configuration in accordance with ACSC guidance, and have enforced separation of work and personal data.

name: ISM-0694
practice_description: Privately-owned mobile devices do not access SECRET and TOP SECRET systems or data.

name: ISM-1482
practice_description: Personnel accessing systems or data using an organisation-owned mobile device use an ASD-approved platform, a security configuration in accordance with ACSC guidance, and have enforced separation of work and personal data.

name: ISM-0869
practice_description: Mobile devices encrypt their internal storage and any removable media.

name: ISM-1085
practice_description: Mobile devices encrypt all sensitive or classified data communicated over public network infrastructure.

name: ISM-1196
practice_description: OFFICIAL and PROTECTED mobile devices are configured to remain undiscoverable to other Bluetooth devices except during Bluetooth pairing.

name: ISM-1200
practice_description: Bluetooth pairing for OFFICIAL and PROTECTED mobile devices is performed using Secure Connections, preferably with Numeric Comparison if supported.

name: ISM-1198
practice_description: Bluetooth pairing for OFFICIAL and PROTECTED mobile devices is performed in a manner such that connections are only made between intended Bluetooth devices.

name: ISM-1199
practice_description: Bluetooth pairings for OFFICIAL and PROTECTED mobile devices are removed when there is no longer a requirement for their use.

name: ISM-0682
practice_description: Bluetooth functionality is not enabled on SECRET and TOP SECRET mobile devices.

name: ISM-0863
practice_description: Mobile devices prevent personnel from installing or uninstalling non-approved applications once provisioned.

name: ISM-0864
practice_description: Mobile devices prevent personnel from disabling or modifying security functionality once provisioned.

name: ISM-1366
practice_description: Security updates are applied to mobile devices as soon as they become available.

capability_description: Connecting mobile devices to the internet
name: ISM-0874
practice_description: Mobile devices access the internet via a VPN connection to an organisation's internet gateway rather than via a direct connection to the internet.

capability_description: Connecting mobile devices to the internet
name: ISM-0705
practice_description: When accessing an organisation's network via a VPN connection, split tunnelling is disabled.

name: ISM-1082
practice_description: A mobile device usage policy is developed, implemented and maintained.

name: ISM-1083
practice_description: Personnel are advised of the sensitivity or classification permitted for voice and data communications when using mobile devices.

capability_description: Paging, message services and messaging apps
name: ISM-0240, message services and messaging apps
practice_description: Paging, Multimedia Message Service, Short Message Service and messaging apps are not used to communicate sensitive or classified data.

capability_description: Using mobile devices in public spaces
name: ISM-0866
practice_description: Sensitive or classified data is not viewed or communicated in public locations unless care is taken to reduce the chance of the screen of a mobile device being observed.

capability_description: Using mobile devices in public spaces
name: ISM-1145
practice_description: Privacy filters are applied to the screens of SECRET and TOP SECRET mobile devices.

capability_description: Using mobile devices in public spaces
name: ISM-1644
practice_description: Sensitive or classified phone calls are not conducted in public locations unless care is taken to reduce the chance of conversations being overheard.

name: ISM-0871
practice_description: Mobile devices are kept under continual direct supervision when being actively used.

name: ISM-0870
practice_description: Mobile devices are carried or stored in a secured state when not being actively used.

name: ISM-1084
practice_description: If unable to carry or store mobile devices in a secured state, they are physically transferred in a security briefcase or an approved multi-use satchel, pouch or transit bag.

capability_description: Mobile device emergency sanitisation processes and procedures
name: ISM-0701
practice_description: Mobile device emergency sanitisation processes, and supporting mobile device emergency sanitisation procedures, are developed, implemented and maintained.

capability_description: Mobile device emergency sanitisation processes and procedures
name: ISM-0702
practice_description: If a cryptographic zeroise or sanitise function is provided for cryptographic keys on a SECRET or TOP SECRET mobile device, the function is used as part of mobile device emergency sanitisation processes and procedures.

capability_description: Before travelling overseas with mobile devices
name: ISM-1298
practice_description: Personnel are advised of privacy and security risks when travelling overseas with mobile devices.

capability_description: Before travelling overseas with mobile devices
name: ISM-1554
practice_description: If travelling overseas with mobile devices to high or extreme risk countries, personnel are: • issued with newly provisioned accounts, mobile devices and removable media from a pool of dedicated travel devices which are used solely for work-related activities • advised on how to apply and inspect tamper seals to key areas of mobile devices • advised to avoid taking any personal mobile devices, especially if rooted or jailbroken.

capability_description: Before travelling overseas with mobile devices
name: ISM-1555
practice_description: Before travelling overseas with mobile devices, personnel take the following actions: • record all details of the mobile devices being taken, such as product types, serial numbers and International Mobile Equipment Identity numbers • update all operating systems and applications • remove all non-essential accounts, applications and data • apply security configuration settings, such as lock screens • configure remote locate and wipe functionality • enable encryption, including for any removable media • backup all important data and configuration settings.

capability_description: While travelling overseas with mobile devices
name: ISM-1299
practice_description: Personnel take the following precautions when travelling overseas with mobile devices: • never leaving mobile devices or removable media unattended for any period of time, including by placing them in checked-in luggage or leaving them in hotel safes • never storing credentials with mobile devices that they grant access to, such as in laptop bags • never lending mobile devices or removable media to untrusted people, even if briefly • never allowing untrusted people to connect their mobile devices or removable media, including for charging • never using designated charging stations, wall outlet charging ports or chargers supplied by untrusted people • avoiding connecting mobile devices to open or untrusted Wi-Fi networks • using a VPN connection to encrypt all mobile device communications • using encrypted messaging apps for communications instead of using foreign telecommunication networks • disabling any communications capabilities of mobile devices when not in use, such as cellular data, wireless, Bluetooth and Near Field Communication • avoiding reuse of removable media once used with other parties' systems or mobile devices • ensuring any removable media used for data transfers are thoroughly checked for malicious code beforehand • never using any gifted mobile devices, especially removable media, when travelling or upon returning from travelling.

capability_description: While travelling overseas with mobile devices
name: ISM-1088
practice_description: Personnel report the potential compromise of mobile devices, removable media or credentials to their organisation as soon as possible, especially if they: • provide credentials to foreign government officials • decrypt mobile devices for foreign government officials • have mobile devices taken out of sight by foreign government officials • have mobile devices or removable media stolen that are later returned • lose mobile devices or removable media that are later found • observe unusual behaviour of mobile devices.

capability_description: After travelling overseas with mobile devices
name: ISM-1300
practice_description: Upon returning from travelling overseas with mobile devices, personnel take the following actions: • sanitise and reset mobile devices, including all removable media • decommission any credentials that left their possession during their travel • report if significant doubt exists as to the integrity of any mobile devices or removable media.

capability_description: After travelling overseas with mobile devices
name: ISM-1556
practice_description: If returning from travelling overseas with mobile devices to high or extreme risk countries, personnel take the following additional actions: • reset credentials used with mobile devices, including those used for remote access to their organisation's systems • monitor accounts for any indicators of compromise, such as failed logon attempts.

name: ISM-0280
practice_description: If procuring an evaluated product, a product that has completed a PP-based evaluation, including against all applicable PP modules, is selected in preference to one that has completed an EAL-based evaluation.

name: ISM-0285
practice_description: Evaluated products are delivered in a manner consistent with any delivery procedures defined in associated evaluation documentation.

name: ISM-0286
practice_description: When procuring high assurance ICT equipment, the ACSC is contacted for any equipment-specific delivery procedures.

capability_description: Installation and configuration of evaluated products
name: ISM-0289
practice_description: Evaluated products are installed, configured, administered and operated in accordance with vendor guidance and evaluation documentation.

capability_description: Installation and configuration of evaluated products
name: ISM-0290
practice_description: High assurance ICT equipment is installed, configured, administered and operated in accordance with guidance produced by the ACSC.

capability_description: Use of high assurance ICT equipment in unevaluated configurations
name: ISM-0292
practice_description: High assurance ICT equipment is always operated in an evaluated configuration.

name: ISM-1551
practice_description: An ICT equipment management policy is developed, implemented and maintained.

name: ISM-0336
practice_description: An ICT equipment register is developed, implemented, maintained and verified on a regular basis.

name: ISM-0294
practice_description: ICT equipment, with the exception of high assurance ICT equipment, is labelled with protective markings reflecting its sensitivity or classification.

name: ISM-0296
practice_description: The Australian Cyber Security Centre (ACSC)'s approval is sought before applying labels to external surfaces of high assurance ICT equipment.

name: ISM-0293
practice_description: ICT equipment is classified based on the highest sensitivity or classification of data that it is approved for processing, storing or communicating.

name: ISM-1599
practice_description: ICT equipment is handled in a manner suitable for its sensitivity or classification.

capability_description: Maintenance and repairs of high assurance ICT equipment
name: ISM-1079
practice_description: The ACSC's approval is sought before undertaking any maintenance or repairs to high assurance ICT equipment.

name: ISM-0305
practice_description: Maintenance and repairs of ICT equipment is carried out on site by an appropriately cleared technician.

name: ISM-0307
practice_description: If an uncleared technician is used to undertake maintenance or repairs of ICT equipment, the ICT equipment and associated media is sanitised before maintenance or repair work is undertaken.

name: ISM-0306
practice_description: If an uncleared technician is used to undertake maintenance or repairs of ICT equipment, the technician is escorted by someone who: • is appropriately cleared and briefed • takes due care to ensure that data is not disclosed • takes all responsible measures to ensure the integrity of the ICT equipment • has the authority to direct the technician • is sufficiently familiar with the ICT equipment to understand the work being performed.

name: ISM-0310
practice_description: ICT equipment maintained or repaired off site is done so at facilities approved for handling the sensitivity or classification of the ICT equipment.

capability_description: Inspection of ICT equipment following maintenance and repairs
name: ISM-1598
practice_description: Following maintenance or repair activities for ICT equipment, the ICT equipment is inspected to confirm it retains its approved software configuration and that no unauthorised modifications have taken place.

capability_description: ICT equipment sanitisation processes and procedures
name: ISM-0313
practice_description: ICT equipment sanitisation processes, and supporting ICT equipment sanitisation procedures, are developed, implemented and maintained.

capability_description: ICT equipment destruction processes and procedures
name: ISM-1741
practice_description: ICT equipment destruction processes, and supporting ICT equipment destruction procedures, are developed, implemented and maintained.

name: ISM-0311
practice_description: ICT equipment containing media is sanitised by removing the media from the ICT equipment or by sanitising the media in situ.

name: ISM-1742
practice_description: ICT equipment that cannot be sanitised is destroyed.

name: ISM-1218
practice_description: ICT equipment, including associated media, that is located overseas and has processed, stored or communicated AUSTEO or AGAO data, is sanitised in situ.

name: ISM-0312
practice_description: ICT equipment, including associated media, that is located overseas and has processed, stored or communicated AUSTEO or AGAO data that cannot be sanitised in situ, is returned to Australia for destruction.

name: ISM-0315
practice_description: High assurance ICT equipment is destroyed prior to its disposal.

name: ISM-0317
practice_description: At least three pages of random text with no blank areas are printed on each colour printer cartridge or MFD print drum.

name: ISM-1219
practice_description: MFD print drums and image transfer rollers are inspected and destroyed if there is remnant toner which cannot be removed or a print is visible on the image transfer roller.

name: ISM-1220
practice_description: Printer and MFD platens are inspected and destroyed if any text or images are retained on the platen.

name: ISM-1221
practice_description: Printers and MFDs are checked to ensure no pages are trapped in the paper path due to a paper jam.

name: ISM-0318
practice_description: When unable to sanitise printer cartridges or MFD print drums, they are destroyed as per electrostatic memory devices.

name: ISM-1534
practice_description: Printer ribbons in printers and MFDs are removed and destroyed.

name: ISM-1076
practice_description: Televisions and computer monitors with minor burn-in or image persistence are sanitised by displaying a solid white image on the screen for an extended period of time.

name: ISM-1222
practice_description: Televisions and computer monitors that cannot be sanitised are destroyed.

name: ISM-1223
practice_description: Memory in network devices is sanitised using the following processes, in order of preference: • following device-specific guidance provided in evaluation documentation • following vendor sanitisation guidance • loading a dummy configuration file, performing a factory reset and then reinstalling firmware.

name: ISM-1225
practice_description: The paper tray of the fax machine is removed, and a fax message with a minimum length of four pages is transmitted, before the paper tray is re-installed to allow a fax summary page to be printed.

name: ISM-1226
practice_description: Fax machines are checked to ensure no pages are trapped in the paper path due to a paper jam.

capability_description: ICT equipment disposal processes and procedures
name: ISM-1550
practice_description: ICT equipment disposal processes, and supporting ICT equipment disposal procedures, are developed, implemented and maintained.

name: ISM-1217
practice_description: Labels and markings indicating the owner, sensitivity, classification or any other marking that can associate ICT equipment with its prior use are removed prior to its disposal.

name: ISM-0321
practice_description: When disposing of ICT equipment that has been designed or modified to meet emanation security standards, the ACSC is contacted for requirements relating to its disposal.

name: ISM-0316
practice_description: Following sanitisation, destruction or declassification, a formal administrative decision is made to release ICT equipment, or its waste, into the public domain.

name: ISM-1549
practice_description: A media management policy is developed, implemented and maintained.

name: ISM-1359
practice_description: A removable media usage policy is developed, implemented and maintained.

name: ISM-1713
practice_description: A removable media register is developed, implemented, maintained and verified on a regular basis.

name: ISM-0332
practice_description: Media, with the exception of internally mounted fixed media within ICT equipment, is labelled with protective markings reflecting its sensitivity or classification.

name: ISM-0323
practice_description: Media is classified to the highest sensitivity or classification of data it stores, unless the media has been classified to a higher sensitivity or classification.

name: ISM-0337
practice_description: Media is only used with systems that are authorised to process, store or communicate its sensitivity or classification.

name: ISM-0325
practice_description: Any media connected to a system with a higher sensitivity or classification than the media is reclassified to the higher sensitivity or classification, unless the media is read-only or the system has a mechanism through which read-only access can be ensured.

name: ISM-0330
practice_description: Before reclassifying media to a lower sensitivity or classification, the media is sanitised or destroyed, and a formal administrative decision is made to reclassify it.

name: ISM-0831
practice_description: Media is handled in a manner suitable for its sensitivity or classification.

name: ISM-1059
practice_description: All data stored on media is encrypted.

name: ISM-1600
practice_description: Media is sanitised before it is used for the first time.

name: ISM-1642
practice_description: Media is sanitised before it is reused in a different security domain.

name: ISM-0347
practice_description: When transferring data manually between two systems belonging to different security domains, write-once media is used unless the destination system has a mechanism through which read-only access can be ensured.

name: ISM-0947
practice_description: When transferring data manually between two systems belonging to different security domains, rewritable media is sanitised after each data transfer.

name: ISM-0348
practice_description: Media sanitisation processes, and supporting media sanitisation procedures, are developed, implemented and maintained.

name: ISM-0351
practice_description: Volatile media is sanitised by removing its power for at least 10 minutes.

name: ISM-0352
practice_description: SECRET and TOP SECRET volatile media is sanitised by overwriting it at least once in its entirety with a random pattern followed by a read back for verification.

capability_description: Treatment of volatile media following sanitisation
name: ISM-0835
practice_description: Following sanitisation, TOP SECRET volatile media retains its classification if it stored static data for an extended period of time, or had data repeatedly stored on or written to the same memory location for an extended period of time.

name: ISM-0354
practice_description: Non-volatile magnetic media is sanitised by overwriting it at least once (or three times if pre-2001 or under 15 GB) in its entirety with a random pattern followed by a read back for verification.

name: ISM-1065
practice_description: The host-protected area and device configuration overlay table are reset prior to the sanitisation of non-volatile magnetic hard drives.

name: ISM-1067
practice_description: The ATA secure erase command is used, in addition to block overwriting software, to ensure the growth defects table of non-volatile magnetic hard drives is overwritten.

capability_description: Treatment of non-volatile magnetic media following sanitisation
name: ISM-0356
practice_description: Following sanitisation, SECRET and TOP SECRET non-volatile magnetic media retains its classification.

capability_description: Non-volatile erasable programmable read-only memory media sanitisation
name: ISM-0357
practice_description: Non-volatile EPROM media is sanitised by applying three times the manufacturer's specified ultraviolet erasure time and then overwriting it at least once in its entirety with a random pattern followed by a read back for verification.

capability_description: Non-volatile electrically erasable programmable read-only memory media sanitisation
name: ISM-0836
practice_description: Non-volatile EEPROM media is sanitised by overwriting it at least once in its entirety with a random pattern followed by a read back for verification.

capability_description: Treatment of non-volatile erasable and electrically erasable programmable read-only memory media following sanitisation
name: ISM-0358
