practice_description: Following sanitisation, SECRET and TOP SECRET non-volatile EPROM and EEPROM media retains its classification.

name: ISM-0359
practice_description: Non-volatile flash memory media is sanitised by overwriting it at least twice in its entirety with a random pattern followed by a read back for verification.

capability_description: Treatment of non-volatile flash memory media following sanitisation
name: ISM-0360
practice_description: Following sanitisation, SECRET and TOP SECRET non-volatile flash memory media retains its classification.

capability_description: Media that cannot be successfully sanitised
name: ISM-1735
practice_description: Faulty or damaged media that cannot be successfully sanitised is destroyed prior to its disposal.

name: ISM-0363
practice_description: Media destruction processes, and supporting media destruction procedures, are developed, implemented and maintained.

name: ISM-0350
practice_description: The following media types are destroyed prior to their disposal: • microfiche and microfilm • optical discs • programmable read-only memory • read-only memory • other types of media that cannot be sanitised.

name: ISM-1361
practice_description: Security Construction and Equipment Committee-approved equipment or ASIO-approved equipment is used when destroying media.

name: ISM-1160
practice_description: If using degaussers to destroy media, degaussers evaluated by the United States' National Security Agency are used.

name: ISM-1517
practice_description: Equipment that is capable of reducing microform to a fine powder, with resultant particles not showing more than five consecutive characters per particle upon microscopic inspection, is used to destroy microfiche and microfilm.

name: ISM-1722
practice_description: Electrostatic memory devices are destroyed using a furnace/incinerator, hammer mill, disintegrator or grinder/sander.

name: ISM-1723
practice_description: Magnetic floppy disks are destroyed using a furnace/incinerator, hammer mill, disintegrator, degausser or by cutting.

name: ISM-1724
practice_description: Magnetic hard disks are destroyed using a furnace/incinerator, hammer mill, disintegrator, grinder/sander or degausser.

name: ISM-1725
practice_description: Magnetic tapes are destroyed using a furnace/incinerator, hammer mill, disintegrator, degausser or by cutting.

name: ISM-1726
practice_description: Optical disks are destroyed using a furnace/incinerator, hammer mill, disintegrator, grinder/sander or by cutting.

name: ISM-1727
practice_description: Semiconductor memory is destroyed using a furnace/incinerator, hammer mill or disintegrator.

name: ISM-0368
practice_description: Media destroyed using a hammer mill, disintegrator, grinder/sander or by cutting results in media waste particles no larger than 9 mm.

name: ISM-1728
practice_description: The resulting media waste particles from the destruction of SECRET media is stored and handled as OFFICIAL if less than or equal to 3 mm, PROTECTED if greater than 3 mm and less than or equal to 6 mm, or SECRET if greater than 6 mm and less than or equal to 9 mm.

name: ISM-1729
practice_description: The resulting media waste particles from the destruction of TOP SECRET media is stored and handled as OFFICIAL if less than or equal to 3 mm, or SECRET if greater than 3 mm and less than or equal to 9 mm.

name: ISM-0361
practice_description: Magnetic media is destroyed using a degausser with a suitable magnetic field strength and magnetic orientation.

name: ISM-0362
practice_description: Product-specific directions provided by degausser manufacturers are followed.

name: ISM-1641
practice_description: Following the use of a degausser, magnetic media is physically damaged by deforming any internal platters.

name: ISM-0370
practice_description: The destruction of media is performed under the supervision of at least one person cleared to its sensitivity or classification.

name: ISM-0371
practice_description: Personnel supervising the destruction of media supervise its handling to the point of destruction and ensure that the destruction is completed successfully.

name: ISM-0372
practice_description: The destruction of media storing accountable material is performed under the supervision of at least two personnel cleared to its sensitivity or classification.

name: ISM-0373
practice_description: Personnel supervising the destruction of media storing accountable material supervise its handling to the point of destruction, ensure that the destruction is completed successfully and sign a destruction certificate afterwards.

name: ISM-0839
practice_description: The destruction of media storing accountable material is not outsourced.

name: ISM-0840
practice_description: When outsourcing the destruction of media storing non-accountable material, a National Association for Information Destruction AAA certified destruction service with endorsements, as specified in ASIO's Protective Security Circular-167, is used.

name: ISM-0374
practice_description: Media disposal processes, and supporting media disposal procedures, are developed, implemented and maintained.

name: ISM-0378
practice_description: Labels and markings indicating the owner, sensitivity, classification or any other marking that can associate media with its prior use are removed prior to its disposal.

name: ISM-0375
practice_description: Following sanitisation, destruction or declassification, a formal administrative decision is made to release media, or its waste, into the public domain.

name: ISM-1743
practice_description: Operating systems are chosen from vendors that have demonstrated a commitment to secure-by-design and secure-by-default principles, use of memory-safe programming languages where possible, secure programming practices, and maintaining the security of their products.

name: ISM-1407
practice_description: The latest release, or the previous release, of operating systems are used.

name: ISM-1408
practice_description: Where supported, 64-bit versions of operating systems are used.

name: ISM-1406
practice_description: SOEs are used for workstations and servers.

name: ISM-1608
practice_description: SOEs provided by third parties are scanned for malicious code and configurations.

name: ISM-1588
practice_description: SOEs are reviewed and updated at least annually.

name: ISM-1409
practice_description: ACSC and vendor guidance is implemented to assist in hardening the configuration of operating systems.

name: ISM-0380
practice_description: Unneeded accounts, components, services and functionality of operating systems are disabled or removed.

name: ISM-0383
practice_description: Default accounts or credentials for operating systems, including for any pre-configured accounts, are changed.

name: ISM-0341
practice_description: Automatic execution features for removable media are disabled.

name: ISM-1654
practice_description: Internet Explorer 11 is disabled or removed.

name: ISM-1655
practice_description: .NET Framework 3.5 (includes .NET 2.0 and 3.0) is disabled or removed.

name: ISM-1492
practice_description: Operating system exploit protection functionality is enabled.

name: ISM-1745
practice_description: Early Launch Antimalware, Secure Boot, Trusted Boot and Measured Boot functionality is enabled.

name: ISM-1584
practice_description: Unprivileged users are prevented from bypassing, disabling or modifying security functionality of operating systems.

name: ISM-1491
practice_description: Unprivileged users are prevented from running script execution engines, including: • Windows Script Host (cscript.exe and wscript.exe) • PowerShell (powershell.exe, powershell_ise.exe and pwsh.exe) • Command Prompt (cmd.exe) • Windows Management Instrumentation (wmic.exe) • Microsoft Hypertext Markup Language (HTML) Application Host (mshta.exe).

name: ISM-1592
practice_description: Unprivileged users do not have the ability to install unapproved software.

name: ISM-0382
practice_description: Unprivileged users do not have the ability to uninstall or disable approved software.

name: ISM-0843
practice_description: Application control is implemented on workstations.
expanded_guidance: ## Application Control can be implemented using Microsoft AppLocker or Windows Defender Application Control (WDAC). ## Guidance on Application Control can be found here: [Implementing Application Control](https://www.cyber.gov.au/resources-business-and-government/maintaining-devices-and-systems/system-hardening-and-administration/system-hardening/implementing-application-control)

name: ISM-1490
practice_description: Application control is implemented on internet-facing servers.
expanded_guidance: ## Application Control can be implemented using Microsoft AppLocker or Windows Defender Application Control (WDAC). ## Guidance on Application Control can be found here: [Implementing Application Control](https://www.cyber.gov.au/resources-business-and-government/maintaining-devices-and-systems/system-hardening-and-administration/system-hardening/implementing-application-control)

name: ISM-1656
practice_description: Application control is implemented on non-internet-facing servers.
expanded_guidance: ## Application Control can be implemented using Microsoft AppLocker or Windows Defender Application Control (WDAC). ## Guidance on Application Control can be found here: [Implementing Application Control](https://www.cyber.gov.au/resources-business-and-government/maintaining-devices-and-systems/system-hardening-and-administration/system-hardening/implementing-application-control)

name: ISM-1657
practice_description: Application control restricts the execution of executables, software libraries, scripts, installers, compiled HTML, HTML applications and control panel applets to an organisation-approved set.

name: ISM-1658
practice_description: Application control restricts the execution of drivers to an organisation-approved set.

name: ISM-0955
practice_description: Application control is implemented using cryptographic hash rules, publisher certificate rules or path rules.

name: ISM-1582
practice_description: Application control rulesets are validated on an annual or more frequent basis.

name: ISM-1471
practice_description: When implementing application control using publisher certificate rules, both publisher names and product names are used.

name: ISM-1392
practice_description: When implementing application control using path rules, only approved users can modify approved files and write to approved folders.

name: ISM-1746
practice_description: When implementing application control using path rules, only approved users can change file system permissions for approved files and folders.

name: ISM-1544
practice_description: Microsoft's 'recommended block rules' are implemented.
expanded_guidance: ## Guidance on Microsoft's 'recommended block rules' can be found here: [Microsoft recommended driver block rules](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/security/application-security/application-control/windows-defender-application-control/design/microsoft-recommended-driver-block-rules)

name: ISM-1659
practice_description: Microsoft's 'recommended driver block rules' are implemented.
expanded_guidance: ## Guidance on Microsoft's 'recommended block rules' can be found here: [Microsoft recommended driver block rules](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/security/application-security/application-control/windows-defender-application-control/design/microsoft-recommended-driver-block-rules)

name: ISM-0846
practice_description: All users (with the exception of local administrator accounts and break glass accounts) cannot disable, bypass or be exempted from application control.

name: ISM-1660
practice_description: Allowed and blocked execution events on workstations are logged.
expanded_guidance: ## Application Control can be implemented using Microsoft AppLocker or Windows Defender Application Control (WDAC). ## Guidance on Application Control can be found here: [Implementing Application Control](https://www.cyber.gov.au/resources-business-and-government/maintaining-devices-and-systems/system-hardening-and-administration/system-hardening/implementing-application-control)

name: ISM-1661
practice_description: Allowed and blocked execution events on internet-facing servers are logged.
expanded_guidance: ## Application Control can be implemented using Microsoft AppLocker or Windows Defender Application Control (WDAC). ## Guidance on Application Control can be found here: [Implementing Application Control](https://www.cyber.gov.au/resources-business-and-government/maintaining-devices-and-systems/system-hardening-and-administration/system-hardening/implementing-application-control)

name: ISM-1662
practice_description: Allowed and blocked execution events on non-internet-facing servers are logged.
expanded_guidance: ## Application Control can be implemented using Microsoft AppLocker or Windows Defender Application Control (WDAC). ## Guidance on Application Control can be found here: [Implementing Application Control](https://www.cyber.gov.au/resources-business-and-government/maintaining-devices-and-systems/system-hardening-and-administration/system-hardening/implementing-application-control)

name: ISM-1663
practice_description: Application control event logs are stored centrally.
expanded_guidance: ## Application Control can be implemented using Microsoft AppLocker or Windows Defender Application Control (WDAC). ## Guidance on Application Control can be found here: [Implementing Application Control](https://www.cyber.gov.au/resources-business-and-government/maintaining-devices-and-systems/system-hardening-and-administration/system-hardening/implementing-application-control)

name: ISM-1621
practice_description: Windows PowerShell 2.0 is disabled or removed.
expanded_guidance: ## Guidance on Powershell Mitigations can be found here: [Securing PowerShell in the Enterprise](https://www.cyber.gov.au/resources-business-and-government/maintaining-devices-and-systems/system-hardening-and-administration/system-administration/securing-powershell-enterprise)

name: ISM-1622
practice_description: PowerShell is configured to use Constrained Language Mode.
expanded_guidance: ## Guidance on Powershell Mitigations can be found here: [Securing PowerShell in the Enterprise](https://www.cyber.gov.au/resources-business-and-government/maintaining-devices-and-systems/system-hardening-and-administration/system-administration/securing-powershell-enterprise)

name: ISM-1623
practice_description: PowerShell is configured to use module logging, script block logging and transcription functionality.
expanded_guidance: ## Guidance on Powershell Mitigations can be found here: [Securing PowerShell in the Enterprise](https://www.cyber.gov.au/resources-business-and-government/maintaining-devices-and-systems/system-hardening-and-administration/system-administration/securing-powershell-enterprise)

name: ISM-1624
practice_description: PowerShell script block logs are protected by Protected Event Logging functionality.
expanded_guidance: ## Guidance on Powershell Mitigations can be found here: [Securing PowerShell in the Enterprise](https://www.cyber.gov.au/resources-business-and-government/maintaining-devices-and-systems/system-hardening-and-administration/system-administration/securing-powershell-enterprise)

name: ISM-1664
practice_description: Blocked PowerShell script execution events are logged.
expanded_guidance: ## Guidance on Powershell Mitigations can be found here: [Securing PowerShell in the Enterprise](https://www.cyber.gov.au/resources-business-and-government/maintaining-devices-and-systems/system-hardening-and-administration/system-administration/securing-powershell-enterprise)

name: ISM-1665
practice_description: PowerShell event logs are stored centrally.
expanded_guidance: ## Guidance on Powershell Mitigations can be found here: [Securing PowerShell in the Enterprise](https://www.cyber.gov.au/resources-business-and-government/maintaining-devices-and-systems/system-hardening-and-administration/system-administration/securing-powershell-enterprise)

name: ISM-1341
practice_description: A HIPS is implemented on workstations.

name: ISM-1034
practice_description: A HIPS is implemented on critical servers and high-value servers.

name: ISM-1416
practice_description: A software firewall is implemented on workstations and servers to restrict inbound and outbound network connections to an organisation-approved set of applications and services.

name: ISM-1417
practice_description: Antivirus software is implemented on workstations and servers with: • signature-based detection functionality enabled and set to a high level • heuristic-based detection functionality enabled and set to a high level • reputation rating functionality enabled • ransomware protection functionality enabled • detection signatures configured to update on at least a daily basis • regular scanning configured for all fixed disks and removable media.

name: ISM-1418
practice_description: If there is no business requirement for reading from removable media and devices, such functionality is disabled via the use of device access control software or by disabling external communication interfaces.

name: ISM-0343
practice_description: If there is no business requirement for writing to removable media and devices, such functionality is disabled via the use of device access control software or by disabling external communication interfaces.

name: ISM-0345
practice_description: External communication interfaces that allow DMA are disabled.

name: ISM-0582
practice_description: The following events are logged for operating systems: • application and operating system crashes and error messages • changes to security policies and system configurations • successful user logons and logoffs, failed user logons and account lockouts • failures, restarts and changes to important processes and services • requests to access internet resources • security product-related events • system startups and shutdowns.

name: ISM-1747
practice_description: Operating system event logs are stored centrally.

name: ISM-0938
practice_description: User applications are chosen from vendors that have demonstrated a commitment to secure-by-design and secure-by-default principles, use of memory-safe programming languages where possible, secure programming practices, and maintaining the security of their products.

name: ISM-1806
practice_description: Default accounts or credentials for user applications, including for any pre-configured accounts, are changed.

name: ISM-1412
practice_description: ACSC or vendor hardening guidance for web browsers, Microsoft Office and PDF software is implemented.

name: ISM-1470
practice_description: Unneeded components, services and functionality of office productivity suites, web browsers, email clients, PDF software and security products are disabled or removed.

name: ISM-1235
practice_description: Add-ons, extensions and plug-ins for office productivity suites, web browsers, email clients, PDF software and security products are restricted to an organisation-approved set.

name: ISM-1667
practice_description: Microsoft Office is blocked from creating child processes.

name: ISM-1668
practice_description: Microsoft Office is blocked from creating executable content.

name: ISM-1669
practice_description: Microsoft Office is blocked from injecting code into other processes.

name: ISM-1542
practice_description: Microsoft Office is configured to prevent activation of Object Linking and Embedding packages.

name: ISM-1823
practice_description: Office productivity suite security settings cannot be changed by users.

name: ISM-1486
practice_description: Web browsers do not process Java from the internet.

name: ISM-1485
practice_description: Web browsers do not process web advertisements from the internet.

name: ISM-1666
practice_description: Internet Explorer 11 does not process content from the internet.

name: ISM-1585
practice_description: Web browser security settings cannot be changed by users.

name: ISM-1670
practice_description: PDF software is blocked from creating child processes.

name: ISM-1824
practice_description: PDF software security settings cannot be changed by users.

name: ISM-1601
practice_description: Microsoft's Attack Surface Reduction rules are implemented.

name: ISM-1748
practice_description: Email client security settings cannot be changed by users.

name: ISM-1825
practice_description: Security product security settings cannot be changed by users.

capability_description: Microsoft Active Directory Domain Services account hardening

capability_description: Microsoft Active Directory Domain Services account hardening
name: ISM-1838

capability_description: Microsoft Active Directory Domain Services account hardening
name: ISM-1839

capability_description: Microsoft Active Directory Domain Services account hardening
name: ISM-1840

capability_description: Microsoft Active Directory Domain Services account hardening
name: ISM-1841

capability_description: Microsoft Active Directory Domain Services account hardening
name: ISM-1842

capability_description: Microsoft Active Directory Domain Services account hardening
name: ISM-1843
practice_description: User accounts with unconstrained delegation are reviewed at least annually, and those without an associated Kerberos SPN or demonstrated business requirement are removed.

capability_description: Microsoft Active Directory Domain Services account hardening
name: ISM-1844
practice_description: Computer accounts that are not Microsoft AD SD domain controllers are not trusted for delegation to services.

capability_description: Microsoft Active Directory Domain Services security group memberships
name: ISM-1620
practice_description: Privileged user accounts are members of the Protected Users security group.

capability_description: Microsoft Active Directory Domain Services security group memberships
name: ISM-1845
practice_description: When a user account is disabled, it is removed from all security group memberships.

capability_description: Microsoft Active Directory Domain Services security group memberships
name: ISM-1846
practice_description: The Pre-Windows 2000 Compatible Access security group does not contain user accounts.

name: ISM-1546
practice_description: Users are authenticated before they are granted access to a system and its resources.

name: ISM-1603
practice_description: Authentication methods susceptible to replay attacks are disabled.

name: ISM-1055
practice_description: LAN Manager and NT LAN Manager authentication methods are disabled.

expanded_guidance: ## Guidance on Multi-factor authentication methods can be found here: [Implementing Multi-Factor Authentication](https://www.cyber.gov.au/resources-business-and-government/maintaining-devices-and-systems/system-hardening-and-administration/system-hardening/implementing-multi-factor-authentication)

practice_description: Multi-factor authentication is enabled by default for non-organisational users (but users can choose to opt out) if they authenticate to an organisation's internet-facing services.
expanded_guidance: ## Guidance on Multi-factor authentication methods can be found here: [Implementing Multi-Factor Authentication](https://www.cyber.gov.au/resources-business-and-government/maintaining-devices-and-systems/system-hardening-and-administration/system-hardening/implementing-multi-factor-authentication)

name: ISM-1401
practice_description: Multi-factor authentication uses either: something users have and something users know, or something users have that is unlocked by something users know or are.
expanded_guidance: ## Guidance on Multi-factor authentication methods can be found here: [Implementing Multi-Factor Authentication](https://www.cyber.gov.au/resources-business-and-government/maintaining-devices-and-systems/system-hardening-and-administration/system-hardening/implementing-multi-factor-authentication)

name: ISM-1682
practice_description: Multi-factor authentication is phishing-resistant.

name: ISM-1559
practice_description: Memorised secrets used for multi-factor authentication are a minimum of 6 characters, unless more stringent requirements apply.
expanded_guidance: ## Guidance on Multi-factor authentication methods can be found here: [Implementing Multi-Factor Authentication](https://www.cyber.gov.au/resources-business-and-government/maintaining-devices-and-systems/system-hardening-and-administration/system-hardening/implementing-multi-factor-authentication)

name: ISM-1560
practice_description: Memorised secrets used for multi-factor authentication on SECRET systems are a minimum of 8 characters.
expanded_guidance: ## Guidance on Multi-factor authentication methods can be found here: [Implementing Multi-Factor Authentication](https://www.cyber.gov.au/resources-business-and-government/maintaining-devices-and-systems/system-hardening-and-administration/system-hardening/implementing-multi-factor-authentication)

name: ISM-1561
practice_description: Memorised secrets used for multi-factor authentication on TOP SECRET systems are a minimum of 10 characters.
expanded_guidance: ## Guidance on Multi-factor authentication methods can be found here: [Implementing Multi-Factor Authentication](https://www.cyber.gov.au/resources-business-and-government/maintaining-devices-and-systems/system-hardening-and-administration/system-hardening/implementing-multi-factor-authentication)

name: ISM-1683

expanded_guidance: ## Guidance on Multi-factor authentication methods can be found here: [Implementing Multi-Factor Authentication](https://www.cyber.gov.au/resources-business-and-government/maintaining-devices-and-systems/system-hardening-and-administration/system-hardening/implementing-multi-factor-authentication)

name: ISM-1684
practice_description: Multi-factor authentication event logs are stored centrally.
expanded_guidance: ## Guidance on Multi-factor authentication methods can be found here: [Implementing Multi-Factor Authentication](https://www.cyber.gov.au/resources-business-and-government/maintaining-devices-and-systems/system-hardening-and-administration/system-hardening/implementing-multi-factor-authentication)

name: ISM-0417
practice_description: When systems cannot support multi-factor authentication, single-factor authentication using passphrases is implemented instead.

name: ISM-0421
practice_description: Passphrases used for single-factor authentication are at least 4 random words with a total minimum length of 14 characters, unless more stringent requirements apply.

name: ISM-1557
practice_description: Passphrases used for single-factor authentication on SECRET systems are at least 5 random words with a total minimum length of 17 characters.

name: ISM-0422
practice_description: Passphrases used for single-factor authentication on TOP SECRET systems are at least 6 random words with a total minimum length of 20 characters.

name: ISM-1558
practice_description: Passphrases used for single-factor authentication are not a list of categorised words; do not form a real sentence in a natural language; and are not constructed from song lyrics, movies, literature or any other publicly available material.

name: ISM-1593
practice_description: Users provide sufficient evidence to verify their identity when requesting new credentials.

name: ISM-1227
practice_description: Credentials set for user accounts are randomly generated.

name: ISM-1594
practice_description: Credentials are provided to users via a secure communications channel or, if not possible, split into two parts with one part provided to users and the other part provided to supervisors.

name: ISM-1595
practice_description: Credentials provided to users are changed on first use.

name: ISM-1596
practice_description: Credentials, in the form of memorised secrets, are not reused by users across different systems.

capability_description: Setting credentials for local administrator accounts and service accounts
name: ISM-1685
practice_description: Credentials for local administrator accounts and service accounts are long, unique, unpredictable and managed.

capability_description: Setting credentials for local administrator accounts and service accounts
name: ISM-1619
practice_description: Service accounts are created as group Managed Service Accounts.

capability_description: Setting credentials for local administrator accounts and service accounts
name: ISM-1795
practice_description: Credentials for local administrator accounts and service accounts are a minimum of 30 characters.

name: ISM-1590
practice_description: Credentials are changed if: • they are directly compromised • they are suspected of being compromised • they appear in an online data breach database • they are discovered stored on networks in the clear • they are discovered being transferred across networks in the clear • membership of a shared account changes • they have not been changed in the past 12 months.

name: ISM-1847
practice_description: Credentials for the Kerberos Key Distribution Center's service account (KRBTGT) are changed twice, allowing for replication to all Microsoft Active Directory Domain Services domain controllers in-between each change, if: • the domain has been directly compromised • the domain is suspected of being compromised • they have not been changed in the past 12 months.

name: ISM-0418
practice_description: Credentials are kept separate from systems they are used to authenticate to, except for when performing authentication activities.

name: ISM-1597
practice_description: Credentials are obscured as they are entered into systems.

name: ISM-1402
practice_description: Credentials stored on systems are protected by a password manager; a hardware security module; or by salting, hashing and stretching them before storage within a database.

name: ISM-1686
practice_description: Windows Defender Credential Guard and Windows Defender Remote Credential Guard are enabled.

name: ISM-1749
practice_description: Cached credentials are limited to one previous logon.

name: ISM-1403
practice_description: Accounts are locked out after a maximum of five failed logon attempts.

name: ISM-0853
practice_description: On a daily basis, outside of business hours and after an appropriate period of inactivity, user sessions are terminated and workstations are restarted.

name: ISM-0428
practice_description: Systems are configured with a session or screen lock that: • activates after a maximum of 15 minutes of user inactivity, or if manually activated by users • conceals all session content on the screen • ensures that the screen does not enter a power saving state before the session or screen lock is activated • requires users to authenticate to unlock the session • denies users the ability to disable the session or screen locking mechanism.

name: ISM-0408
practice_description: Systems have a logon banner that requires users to acknowledge and accept their security responsibilities before access is granted.

name: ISM-0979
practice_description: Legal advice is sought on the exact wording of logon banners.

name: ISM-1460
practice_description: When using a software-based isolation mechanism to share a physical server's hardware, the isolation mechanism is from a vendor that has demonstrated a commitment to secure-by-design and secure-by-default principles, use of memory-safe programming languages where possible, secure programming practices, and maintaining the security of their products.

name: ISM-1604
practice_description: When using a software-based isolation mechanism to share a physical server's hardware, the configuration of the isolation mechanism is hardened by removing unneeded functionality and restricting access to the administrative interface used to manage the isolation mechanism.

name: ISM-1605
practice_description: When using a software-based isolation mechanism to share a physical server's hardware, the underlying operating system is hardened.

name: ISM-1606
practice_description: When using a software-based isolation mechanism to share a physical server's hardware, patches, updates or vendor mitigations for security vulnerabilities are applied to the isolation mechanism and underlying operating system in a timely manner.

name: ISM-1848
practice_description: When using a software-based isolation mechanism to share a physical server's hardware, the isolation mechanism or underlying operating system is replaced when it is no longer supported by a vendor.

name: ISM-1607
practice_description: When using a software-based isolation mechanism to share a physical server's hardware, integrity and log monitoring are performed for the isolation mechanism and underlying operating system in a timely manner.

name: ISM-1461
practice_description: When using a software-based isolation mechanism to share a physical server's hardware for SECRET or TOP SECRET computing environments, the physical server and all computing environments are of the same classification and belong to the same security domain.

name: ISM-0042
practice_description: System administration processes, and supporting system administration procedures, are developed, implemented and maintained.

name: ISM-1211
practice_description: System administrators document requirements for administrative activities, consider potential security impacts, obtain any necessary approvals, notify users of any disruptions or outages, and maintain system and security documentation.

name: ISM-1380
practice_description: Privileged users use separate privileged and unprivileged operating environments.

name: ISM-1687
practice_description: Privileged operating environments are not virtualised within unprivileged operating environments.

name: ISM-1688
practice_description: Unprivileged accounts cannot logon to privileged operating environments.

name: ISM-1689
practice_description: Privileged accounts (excluding local administrator accounts) cannot logon to unprivileged operating environments.

name: ISM-1385
practice_description: Administrative infrastructure is segregated from the wider network.

name: ISM-1750
practice_description: Administrative infrastructure for critical servers, high-value servers and regular servers is segregated from each other.

name: ISM-1386
practice_description: Network management traffic can only originate from administrative infrastructure.

name: ISM-1387
practice_description: Administrative activities are conducted through jump servers.

name: ISM-1381
practice_description: Only privileged operating environments can communicate with jump servers.

name: ISM-1388
practice_description: Only jump servers can communicate with assets requiring administrative activities to be performed.
