capability_description: Data backup and restoration processes and procedures
name: ISM-1547
practice_description: Data backup processes, and supporting data backup procedures, are developed, implemented and maintained.

capability_description: Data backup and restoration processes and procedures
name: ISM-1548
practice_description: Data restoration processes, and supporting data restoration procedures, are developed, implemented and maintained.

name: ISM-1511
practice_description: Backups of important data, software and configuration settings are performed and retained with a frequency and retention timeframe in accordance with business continuity requirements.

name: ISM-1810
practice_description: Backups of important data, software and configuration settings are synchronised to enable restoration to a common point in time.

name: ISM-1811
practice_description: Backups of important data, software and configuration settings are retained in a secure and resilient manner.

name: ISM-1812
practice_description: Unprivileged accounts cannot access backups belonging to other accounts.

name: ISM-1813
practice_description: Unprivileged accounts cannot access their own backups.

name: ISM-1705
practice_description: Privileged accounts (excluding backup administrator accounts) cannot access backups belonging to other accounts.

name: ISM-1706
practice_description: Privileged accounts (excluding backup administrator accounts) cannot access their own backups.

name: ISM-1814
practice_description: Unprivileged accounts are prevented from modifying and deleting backups.

name: ISM-1707
practice_description: Privileged accounts (excluding backup administrator accounts) are prevented from modifying and deleting backups.

name: ISM-1708
practice_description: Privileged accounts (including backup administrator accounts) are prevented from modifying and deleting backups during their retention period.

name: ISM-1515
practice_description: Restoration of important data, software and configuration settings from backups to a common point of time is tested as part of disaster recovery exercises.

name: ISM-0580
practice_description: An event logging policy is developed, implemented and maintained.

name: ISM-0585
practice_description: For each event logged, the date and time of the event, the relevant user or process, the relevant filename, the event description, and the ICT equipment involved are recorded.

name: ISM-1405
practice_description: A centralised event logging facility is implemented and event logs are sent to the facility as soon as possible after they occur.

name: ISM-1815
practice_description: Event logs stored within a centralised event logging facility are protected from unauthorised modification and deletion.

name: ISM-0988
practice_description: An accurate time source is established and used consistently across systems to assist with identifying connections between events.

name: ISM-0109
practice_description: Event logs are analysed in a timely manner to detect cyber security events.

name: ISM-1228
practice_description: Cyber security events are analysed in a timely manner to identify cyber security incidents.

name: ISM-0859
practice_description: Event logs, excluding those for Domain Name System services and web proxies, are retained for at least seven years.

name: ISM-0991
practice_description: Event logs for Domain Name System services and web proxies are retained for at least 18 months.

name: ISM-0400, testing and production environments
practice_description: Development, testing and production environments are segregated.

name: ISM-1419, testing and production environments
practice_description: Development and modification of software only takes place in development environments.

name: ISM-1420, testing and production environments
practice_description: Data from production environments is not used in a development or testing environment unless the environment is secured to the same level as the production environment.

name: ISM-1422, testing and production environments
practice_description: Unauthorised access to the authoritative source for software is prevented.

name: ISM-1816, testing and production environments
practice_description: Unauthorised modification of the authoritative source for software is prevented.

name: ISM-0401
practice_description: Secure-by-design and secure-by-default principles, use of memory-safe programming languages where possible, and secure programming practices are used as part of application development.

name: ISM-1780
practice_description: SecDevOps practices are used for application development.

name: ISM-1238
practice_description: Threat modelling is used in support of application development.

name: ISM-1796
practice_description: Files containing executable content are digitally signed as part of application development.

name: ISM-1797
practice_description: Installers, patches and updates are digitally signed or provided with cryptographic checksums as part of application development.

name: ISM-1798
practice_description: Secure configuration guidance is produced as part of application development.

name: ISM-1730
practice_description: A software bill of materials is produced and made available to consumers of software.

name: ISM-0402
practice_description: Applications are comprehensively tested for security vulnerabilities, using both static application security testing and dynamic application security testing, prior to their initial release and any subsequent releases.

name: ISM-1754
practice_description: Security vulnerabilities identified in applications are resolved by software developers.

name: ISM-1616
practice_description: A vulnerability disclosure program is implemented to assist with the secure development and maintenance of products and services.

name: ISM-1755
practice_description: A vulnerability disclosure policy is developed, implemented and maintained.

name: ISM-1756
practice_description: Vulnerability disclosure processes, and supporting vulnerability disclosure procedures, are developed, implemented and maintained.

name: ISM-1717
practice_description: A ‘security.txt' file is hosted for all internet-facing organisational domains to assist in the responsible disclosure of security vulnerabilities in an organisation's products and services.

name: ISM-0971
practice_description: The OWASP Application Security Verification Standard is used in the development of web applications.

name: ISM-1849
practice_description: The OWASP Top Ten Proactive Controls are used in the development of web applications.

name: ISM-1850
practice_description: The OWASP Top 10 are mitigated in the development of web applications.

name: ISM-1239
practice_description: Robust web application frameworks are used in the development of web applications.

name: ISM-1552
practice_description: All web application content is offered exclusively using HTTPS.

name: ISM-1817
practice_description: Authentication and authorisation of clients is performed when clients call web APIs that facilitate access to data not authorised for release into the public domain.

name: ISM-1818
practice_description: Authentication and authorisation of clients is performed when clients call web APIs that facilitate modification of data.

name: ISM-1851
practice_description: The OWASP API Security Top 10 are mitigated in the development of web APIs.

name: ISM-1240
practice_description: Validation or sanitisation is performed on all input handled by web applications.

name: ISM-1241
practice_description: Output encoding is performed on all output produced by web applications.

name: ISM-1424
practice_description: Web applications implement Content-Security-Policy, HSTS and X-Frame-Options via security policy in response headers.

name: ISM-1536
practice_description: The following events are logged for web applications: attempted access that is denied, crashes and error messages, and search queries initiated by users.

name: ISM-1757
practice_description: Web application event logs are stored centrally.

capability_description: Functional separation between database servers and web servers
name: ISM-1269
practice_description: Database servers and web servers are functionally separated.

capability_description: Communications between database servers and web servers
name: ISM-1277
practice_description: Data communicated between database servers and web servers is encrypted.

name: ISM-1270
practice_description: Database servers are placed on a different network segment to user workstations.

name: ISM-1271
practice_description: Network access controls are implemented to restrict database server communications to strictly defined network resources, such as web servers, application servers and storage area networks.

name: ISM-1272
practice_description: If only local access to a database is required, networking functionality of database management system software is disabled or directed to listen solely to the localhost interface.

capability_description: Separation of development, testing and production database servers
name: ISM-1273, testing and production database servers
practice_description: Development and testing environments do not use the same database servers as production environments.

name: ISM-1243
practice_description: A database register is developed, implemented, maintained and verified on a regular basis.

name: ISM-1256
practice_description: File-based access controls are applied to database files.

name: ISM-0393
practice_description: Databases and their contents are classified based on the sensitivity or classification of data that they contain.

name: ISM-1255
practice_description: Database users' ability to access, insert, modify and remove database contents is restricted based on their work duties.

name: ISM-1268
practice_description: The need-to-know principle is enforced for database contents through the application of minimum privileges, database views and database roles.

capability_description: Separation of development, testing and production databases
name: ISM-1274, testing and production databases
practice_description: Database contents from production environments are not used in development or testing environments unless the environment is secured to the same level as the production environment.

name: ISM-1275
practice_description: All queries to databases from web applications are filtered for legitimate content and correct syntax.

name: ISM-1276
practice_description: Parameterised queries or stored procedures, instead of dynamically generated queries, are used for database interactions.

name: ISM-1278
practice_description: Web applications are designed or configured to provide as little error information as possible about the structure of databases.

name: ISM-1537
practice_description: The following events are logged for databases: • access or modification of particularly important content • addition of new users, especially privileged users • changes to user roles or privileges • attempts to elevate user privileges • queries containing comments • queries containing multiple embedded queries • database and query alerts or failures • database structure changes • database administrator actions • use of executable commands • database logons and logoffs.

name: ISM-1758
practice_description: Database event logs are stored centrally.

name: ISM-0264
practice_description: An email usage policy is developed, implemented and maintained.

name: ISM-0267
practice_description: Access to non-approved webmail services is blocked.

name: ISM-0270
practice_description: Protective markings are applied to emails and reflect the highest sensitivity or classification of the subject, body and attachments.

name: ISM-0271
practice_description: Protective marking tools do not automatically insert protective markings into emails.

name: ISM-0272
practice_description: Protective marking tools do not allow users to select protective markings that a system has not been authorised to process, store or communicate.

name: ISM-1089
practice_description: Protective marking tools do not allow users replying to or forwarding emails to select protective markings lower than previously used.

capability_description: Handling emails with inappropriate, invalid or missing protective markings
name: ISM-0565, invalid or missing protective markings
practice_description: Email servers are configured to block, log and report emails with inappropriate protective markings.

capability_description: Handling emails with inappropriate, invalid or missing protective markings
name: ISM-1023, invalid or missing protective markings
practice_description: The intended recipients of blocked inbound emails, and the senders of blocked outbound emails, are notified.

name: ISM-0269
practice_description: Emails containing Australian Eyes Only, Australian Government Access Only or Releasable To data are not sent to email distribution lists unless the nationality of all members of email distribution lists can be confirmed.

name: ISM-0569
practice_description: Emails are routed via centralised email gateways.

name: ISM-0571
practice_description: When users send or receive emails, an authenticated and encrypted channel is used to route emails via their organisation's centralised email gateways.

name: ISM-0570
practice_description: Where backup or alternative email gateways are in place, they are maintained at the same standard as the primary email gateway.

name: ISM-0567
practice_description: Email servers only relay emails destined for or originating from their domains (including subdomains).

name: ISM-0572
practice_description: Opportunistic TLS encryption is enabled on email servers that make incoming or outgoing email connections over public network infrastructure.

name: ISM-1589
practice_description: MTA-STS is enabled to prevent the unencrypted transfer of emails between complying servers.

name: ISM-0574
practice_description: SPF is used to specify authorised email servers (or lack thereof) for all domains (including subdomains).

name: ISM-1183
practice_description: A hard fail SPF record is used when specifying authorised email servers (or lack thereof) for all domains (including subdomains).

name: ISM-1151
practice_description: SPF is used to verify the authenticity of incoming emails.

name: ISM-0861
practice_description: DKIM signing is enabled on emails originating from an organisation's domains (including subdomains).

name: ISM-1026
practice_description: DKIM signatures on received emails are verified.

name: ISM-1027
practice_description: Email distribution list software used by external senders is configured such that it does not break the validity of the sender's DKIM signature.

capability_description: Domain-based Message Authentication, Reporting and Conformance
name: ISM-1540, Reporting and Conformance
practice_description: DMARC records are configured for all domains (including subdomains) such that emails are rejected if they do not pass DMARC checks.

capability_description: Domain-based Message Authentication, Reporting and Conformance
name: ISM-1799, Reporting and Conformance
practice_description: Incoming emails are rejected if they do not pass DMARC checks.

name: ISM-1234
practice_description: Email content filtering is implemented to filter potentially harmful content in email bodies and attachments.

name: ISM-1502
practice_description: Emails arriving via an external connection where the email source address uses an internal domain, or internal subdomain, are blocked at the email gateway.

name: ISM-1024
practice_description: tifications of undeliverable emails are only sent to senders that can be verified via SPF or other trusted means.

name: ISM-0518
practice_description: Network documentation is developed, implemented, maintained.

name: ISM-0516
practice_description: Network documentation includes high-level network diagrams showing all connections into networks and logical network diagrams showing all critical servers, high-value servers, network devices and network security appliances.

name: ISM-1178
practice_description: Network documentation provided to a third party, or published in public tender documentation, only contains details necessary for other parties to undertake contractual services.

name: ISM-1781
practice_description: All data communicated over network infrastructure is encrypted.

name: ISM-1181
practice_description: Networks are segregated into multiple network zones according to the criticality of servers, services and data.

name: ISM-1577
practice_description: An organisation's networks are segregated from their service providers' networks.

name: ISM-1532
practice_description: VLANs are not used to separate network traffic between an organisation's networks and public network infrastructure.

name: ISM-0529
practice_description: VLANs are not used to separate network traffic between networks belonging to different security domains.

name: ISM-0530
practice_description: Network devices managing VLANs are administered from the most trusted security domain.

name: ISM-0535
practice_description: Network devices managing VLANs belonging to different security domains do not share VLAN trunks.

name: ISM-1364
practice_description: Network devices managing VLANs terminate VLANs belonging to different security domains on separate physical network interfaces.

name: ISM-0521
practice_description: IPv6 functionality is disabled in dual-stack network devices unless it is being used.

name: ISM-1186
practice_description: IPv6 capable network security appliances are used on IPv6 and dual-stack networks.

name: ISM-1428
practice_description: Unless explicitly required, IPv6 tunnelling is disabled on all network devices.

name: ISM-1429
practice_description: IPv6 tunnelling is blocked by network security appliances at externally-connected network boundaries.

name: ISM-1430
practice_description: Dynamically assigned IPv6 addresses are configured with Dynamic Host Configuration Protocol version 6 in a stateful manner with lease data stored in a centralised event logging facility.

name: ISM-0520
practice_description: Network access controls are implemented on networks to prevent the connection of unauthorised network devices.

name: ISM-1182
practice_description: Network access controls are implemented to limit network traffic within and between network segments to only those required for business purposes.

name: ISM-0385
practice_description: Servers maintain effective functional separation with other servers allowing them to operate independently.

name: ISM-1479
practice_description: Servers minimise communications with other servers at both the network and file system level.

name: ISM-1006
practice_description: Security measures are implemented to prevent unauthorised access to network management traffic.

capability_description: Use of Simple Network Management Protocol
name: ISM-1311
practice_description: SNMP version 1 and SNMP version 2 are not used on networks.

capability_description: Use of Simple Network Management Protocol
name: ISM-1312
practice_description: All default SNMP community strings on network devices are changed and write access is disabled.

capability_description: Using Network-based Intrusion Detection and Prevention Systems
name: ISM-1028
practice_description: A NIDS or NIPS is deployed in gateways between an organisation's networks and other networks they do not manage.

capability_description: Using Network-based Intrusion Detection and Prevention Systems
name: ISM-1030
practice_description: A NIDS or NIPS is located immediately inside the outermost firewall for gateways and configured to generate event logs and alerts for network traffic that contravenes any rule in a firewall ruleset.

name: ISM-1627
practice_description: Inbound network connections from anonymity networks to internet-facing services are blocked.

name: ISM-1628
practice_description: Outbound network connections to anonymity networks are blocked.

name: ISM-1782
practice_description: A protective DNS service is used to block access to known malicious domain names.

capability_description: Flashing network devices with trusted firmware before first use
name: ISM-1800
practice_description: Network devices are flashed with trusted firmware before they are used for the first time.

capability_description: Default accounts and credentials for network devices
name: ISM-1304
practice_description: Default accounts or credentials for network devices including for any pre-configured accounts, are changed.

capability_description: Disabling unused physical ports on network devices
name: ISM-0534
practice_description: Unused physical ports on network devices are disabled.

name: ISM-1801
practice_description: Network devices are restarted on at least a monthly basis.

name: ISM-1314
practice_description: All wireless devices are Wi-Fi Alliance certified.

name: ISM-0536
practice_description: Public wireless networks provided for general public use are segregated from all other organisation networks.

capability_description: Administrative interfaces for wireless access points
name: ISM-1315
practice_description: The administrative interface on wireless access points is disabled for wireless network connections.

name: ISM-1710
practice_description: Configuration settings for wireless access points are hardened.

name: ISM-1316
practice_description: Default SSIDs of wireless access points are changed.

name: ISM-1317
practice_description: SSIDs of non-public wireless networks are not readily associated with an organisation, the location of their premises or the functionality of wireless networks.

name: ISM-1318
practice_description: SSID broadcasting is not disabled on wireless access points.

name: ISM-1320
practice_description: MAC address filtering is not used to restrict which devices can connect to wireless networks.

name: ISM-1319
practice_description: Static addressing is not used for assigning IP addresses on wireless networks.

capability_description: Confidentiality and integrity of wireless network traffic
name: ISM-1332
practice_description: WPA3-Enterprise 192-bit mode is used to protect the confidentiality and integrity of all wireless network traffic.

name: ISM-1321
practice_description: 802.1X authentication with EAP-TLS, using X.509 certificates, is used for mutual authentication; with all other EAP methods disabled on supplications and authentication servers.

name: ISM-1711
practice_description: User identity confidentiality is used if available with EAP-TLS implementations.

name: ISM-1322
practice_description: Evaluated supplicants, authenticators, wireless access points and authentication servers are used in wireless networks.

capability_description: Generating and issuing certificates for authentication
name: ISM-1324
practice_description: Certificates are generated using an evaluated certificate authority or hardware security module.

capability_description: Generating and issuing certificates for authentication
name: ISM-1323
practice_description: Certificates are required for both devices and users accessing wireless networks.

capability_description: Generating and issuing certificates for authentication
name: ISM-1327
practice_description: Certificates are protected by encryption, user authentication, and both logical and physical access controls.

name: ISM-1330
practice_description: The PMK caching period is not set to greater than 1440 minutes (24 hours).

name: ISM-1712
practice_description: The use of FT (802.11r) is disabled unless authenticator-to-authenticator communications are secured by an ASD-Approved Cryptographic Protocol.

capability_description: Remote Authentication Dial-In User Service authentication
name: ISM-1454
practice_description: Communications between authenticators and a RADIUS server are encapsulated with an additional layer of encryption using RADIUS over Internet Protocol Security or RADIUS over Transport Layer Security.

name: ISM-1334
practice_description: Wireless networks implement sufficient frequency separation from other wireless networks.

capability_description: Protecting management frames on wireless networks
name: ISM-1335
practice_description: Wireless access points enable the use of the 802.11w amendment to protect management frames.

name: ISM-1338
practice_description: Instead of deploying a small number of wireless access points that broadcast on high power, a greater number of wireless access points that use less broadcast power are deployed to achieve the desired footprint for wireless networks.

name: ISM-1013
practice_description: The effective range of wireless communications outside an organisation's area of control is limited by implementing RF shielding on facilities in which SECRET or TOP SECRET wireless networks are used.

name: ISM-1437
practice_description: Cloud service providers are used for hosting online services.

name: ISM-1578
practice_description: An organisation is notified by cloud service providers of any change to configured regions or availability zones for online services.

capability_description: Availability planning and monitoring for online services
name: ISM-1579
practice_description: Cloud service providers' ability to dynamically scale resources due to a genuine spike in demand or a denial-of-service attack is tested as part of capacity planning processes for online services.

capability_description: Availability planning and monitoring for online services
name: ISM-1580
practice_description: Where a high availability requirement exists for online services, the services are architected to automatically transition between availability zones.

capability_description: Availability planning and monitoring for online services
name: ISM-1441
practice_description: Where a requirement for high availability exists for online services, a denial of service mitigation service is used.

capability_description: Availability planning and monitoring for online services
name: ISM-1581
practice_description: Continuous real-time monitoring of the availability of online services is performed.

name: ISM-1438
practice_description: Where a high availability requirement exists for website hosting, CDNs that cache websites are used.

name: ISM-1439
practice_description: If using CDNs, disclosing the IP addresses of web servers under an organisation's control (referred to as origin servers) is avoided and access to the origin servers is restricted to the CDNs and authorised management networks.

name: ISM-1431
practice_description: Denial-of-service attack mitigation strategies are discussed with cloud service providers, specifically: • their capacity to withstand denial-of-service attacks • any costs likely to be incurred as a result of denial-of-service attacks • thresholds for notification of denial-of-service attacks • thresholds for turning off online services during denial-of-service attacks • pre-approved actions that can be undertaken during denial-of-service attacks • any arrangements with upstream service providers to block malicious network traffic as far upstream as possible.

name: ISM-1458
practice_description: The functionality and quality of online services, how to maintain such functionality, and what functionality can be lived without during a denial-of-service attack, are determined and documented.

name: ISM-1432
practice_description: Domain names for online services are protected via registrar locking and confirming domain registration details are correct.

capability_description: Monitoring with real-time alerting for online services
name: ISM-1435
practice_description: Availability monitoring with real-time alerting is implemented for online services to detect denial-of-service attacks and measure their impact.

name: ISM-1436
practice_description: Critical online services are segregated from other online services that are more likely to be targeted.

name: ISM-1518
practice_description: A static version of a website is pre-prepared that requires minimal processing and bandwidth in order to facilitate at least a basic level of service when under a denial-of-service attack.

name: ISM-1802
practice_description: HACE does not process, store or communicate SECRET or TOP SECRET data until approved for use by ASD.

name: ISM-0499
practice_description: All communications security and equipment-specific doctrine produced by the ACSC for the management and use of ASD-approved HACE is complied with.

capability_description: Cryptographic key management processes and procedures
name: ISM-0507
practice_description: Cryptographic key management processes, and supporting cryptographic key management procedures, are developed, implemented and maintained.

name: ISM-1080
practice_description: An ASD-Approved Cryptographic Algorithm (AACA) or high assurance cryptographic algorithm is used when encrypting media.

name: ISM-0457
practice_description: Cryptographic equipment or software that has completed a Common Criteria evaluation against a Protection Profile is used when encrypting media that contains OFFICIAL: Sensitive or PROTECTED data.

name: ISM-0460
practice_description: ASD-approved HACE is used when encrypting media that contains SECRET or TOP SECRET data.

name: ISM-0459
practice_description: Full disk encryption, or partial encryption where access controls will only allow writing to the encrypted partition, is implemented when encrypting data at rest.

name: ISM-0469
practice_description: An ASD-Approved Cryptographic Protocol (AACP) or high assurance cryptographic protocol is used to protect data when communicated over network infrastructure.

name: ISM-0465
practice_description: Cryptographic equipment or software that has completed a Common Criteria evaluation against a Protection Profile is used to protect OFFICIAL: Sensitive or PROTECTED data when communicated over insufficiently secure networks, outside of appropriately secure areas or via public network infrastructure.

name: ISM-0467
practice_description: ASD-approved HACE is used to protect SECRET and TOP SECRET data when communicated over insufficiently secure networks, outside of appropriately secure areas or via public network infrastructure.

name: ISM-0455
practice_description: Where practical, cryptographic equipment and software provides a means of data recovery to allow for circumstances where the encryption key is unavailable due to loss, damage or failure.

capability_description: Handling encrypted ICT equipment and media
name: ISM-0462
practice_description: When a user authenticates to the encryption functionality of ICT equipment or media, it is treated in accordance with its original sensitivity or classification until the user deauthenticates from the encryption functionality.

name: ISM-0501
practice_description: Keyed cryptographic equipment is transported based on the sensitivity or classification of its keying material.

name: ISM-0142
practice_description: The compromise or suspected compromise of cryptographic equipment or associated keying material is reported to an organisation's Chief Information Security Officer, or one of their delegates, as soon as possible after it occurs.

name: ISM-1091
practice_description: Keying material is changed when compromised or suspected of being compromised.

name: ISM-0471
practice_description: Only AACAs or high assurance cryptographic algorithms are used by cryptographic equipment and software.

name: ISM-0994
practice_description: ECDH and ECDSA are used in preference to DH and DSA.

name: ISM-0472
practice_description: When using DH for agreeing on encryption session keys, a modulus of at least 2048 bits is used, preferably 3072 bits.

name: ISM-1759
practice_description: When using DH for agreeing on encryption session keys, a modulus of at least 3072 bits is used, preferably 3072 bits.

name: ISM-1629
practice_description: When using DH for agreeing on encryption session keys, a modulus and associated parameters are selected according to NIST SP 800-56A Rev. 3.

name: ISM-0473
practice_description: When using DSA for digital signatures, a modulus of at least 2048 bits is used.

name: ISM-1630
practice_description: When using DSA for digital signatures, a modulus and associated parameters are generated according to FIPS 186-4.

name: ISM-1760
practice_description: DSA is not used for digital signatures.

name: ISM-1446
practice_description: When using elliptic curve cryptography, a curve from FIPS 186-4 is used.

name: ISM-0474
practice_description: When using ECDH for agreeing on encryption session keys, a base point order and key size of at least 224 bits is used, preferably the NIST P-384 curve.

name: ISM-1761
practice_description: When using ECDH for agreeing on encryption session keys, NIST P-256, P-384 or P-521 curves are used, preferably the NIST P-384 curve.

name: ISM-1762
practice_description: When using ECDH for agreeing on encryption session keys, NIST P-384 or P-521 curves are used, preferably the NIST P-384 curve.

capability_description: Using the Elliptic Curve Digital Signature Algorithm
name: ISM-0475
practice_description: When using ECDSA for digital signatures, a base point order and key size of at least 224 bits is used, preferably the P-384 curve.

capability_description: Using the Elliptic Curve Digital Signature Algorithm
name: ISM-1763
practice_description: When using ECDSA for digital signatures, NIST P-256, P-384 or P-521 curves are used, preferably the NIST P-384 curve.

capability_description: Using the Elliptic Curve Digital Signature Algorithm
name: ISM-1764
practice_description: When using ECDSA for digital signatures, NIST P-384 or P-521 curves are used, preferably the NIST P-384 curve.

name: ISM-0476
practice_description: When using RSA for digital signatures, and passing encryption session keys or similar keys, a modulus of at least 2048 bits is used, preferably 3072 bits.

name: ISM-1765
practice_description: When using RSA for digital signatures, and passing encryption session keys or similar keys, a modulus of at least 3072 bits is used, preferably 3072 bits.

name: ISM-0477
practice_description: When using RSA for digital signatures, and for passing encryption session keys or similar keys, a different key pair is used for digital signatures and passing encrypted session keys.

name: ISM-1766
practice_description: When using SHA-2 for hashing, an output size of at least 224 bits is used, preferably SHA-384.

name: ISM-1767
practice_description: When using SHA-2 for hashing, an output size of at least 256 bits is used, preferably SHA-384.

name: ISM-1768
practice_description: When using SHA-2 for hashing, an output size of at least 384 bits is used, preferably SHA-384.

name: ISM-1769
practice_description: When using AES for encryption, AES-128, AES-192 or AES-256 is used, preferably AES-256.

name: ISM-1770
practice_description: When using AES for encryption, AES-192 or AES-256 is used, preferably AES-256.

name: ISM-0479
practice_description: Symmetric cryptographic algorithms are not used in Electronic Codebook Mode.

name: ISM-0481
practice_description: Only AACPs or high assurance cryptographic protocols are used by cryptographic equipment and software.

name: ISM-1139
practice_description: Only the latest version of TLS is used for TLS connections.

name: ISM-1369
practice_description: AES-GCM is used for encryption of TLS connections.

name: ISM-1370
practice_description: Only server-initiated secure renegotiation is used for TLS connections.

name: ISM-1372
practice_description: DH or ECDH is used for key establishment of TLS connections.

name: ISM-1448
practice_description: When using DH or ECDH for key establishment of TLS connections, the ephemeral variant is used.

name: ISM-1373
practice_description: Anonymous DH is not used for TLS connections.

name: ISM-1374
practice_description: SHA-2-based certificates are used for TLS connections.

name: ISM-1375
practice_description: SHA-2 is used for the Hash-based Message Authentication Code (HMAC) and pseudorandom function (PRF) for TLS connections.

name: ISM-1553
practice_description: TLS compression is disabled for TLS connections.

name: ISM-1453
practice_description: Perfect Forward Secrecy (PFS) is used for TLS connections.

name: ISM-1506
practice_description: The use of SSH version 1 is disabled for SSH connections.

name: ISM-0484
practice_description: The SSH daemon is configured to: • only listen on the required interfaces (ListenAddress xxx.xxx.xxx.xxx) • have a suitable login banner (Banner x) • have a login authentication timeout of no more than 60 seconds (LoginGraceTime 60) • disable host-based authentication (HostbasedAuthentication no) • disable rhosts-based authentication (IgnoreRhosts yes) • disable the ability to login directly as root (PermitRootLogin no) • disable empty passwords (PermitEmptyPasswords no) • disable connection forwarding (AllowTCPForwarding no) • disable gateway ports (GatewayPorts no) • disable X11 forwarding (X11Forwarding no).

name: ISM-0485
practice_description: Public key-based authentication is used for SSH connections.

name: ISM-1449
practice_description: SSH private keys are protected with a passphrase or a key encryption key.

name: ISM-0487
practice_description: When using logins without a passphrase for SSH connections, the following are disabled: • access from IP addresses that do not require access • port forwarding • agent credential forwarding • X11 display remoting • console access.

name: ISM-0488
practice_description: If using remote access without the use of a passphrase for SSH connections, the ‘forced command' option is used to specify what command is executed and parameter checking is enabled.

name: ISM-0489
practice_description: When SSH-agent or similar key caching programs are used, it is limited to workstations and servers with screen locks and key caches that are set to expire within four hours of inactivity.

name: ISM-0490
practice_description: Versions of S/MIME earlier than S/MIME version 3.0 are not used for S/MIME connections.

name: ISM-0494
practice_description: Tunnel mode is used for IPsec connections; however, if using transport mode, an IP tunnel is used.

name: ISM-0496
practice_description: The ESP protocol is used for authentication and encryption of IPsec connections.

name: ISM-1233
practice_description: IKE version 2 is used for key exchange when establishing IPsec connections.

name: ISM-1771
practice_description: AES is used for encrypting IPsec connections, preferably ENCR_AES_GCM_16.

name: ISM-1772
practice_description: PRF_HMAC_SHA2_256, PRF_HMAC_SHA2_384 or PRF_HMAC_SHA2_512 is used for IPsec connections, preferably PRF_HMAC_SHA2_512.

name: ISM-0998
practice_description: AUTH_HMAC_SHA2_256_128, AUTH_HMAC_SHA2_384_192, AUTH_HMAC_SHA2_512_256 or NONE (only with AES-GCM) is used for authenticating IPsec connections, preferably NONE.

name: ISM-0999
practice_description: DH or ECDH is used for key establishment of IPsec connections, preferably 384-bit random ECP group, 3072-bit MODP Group or 4096-bit MODP Group.

name: ISM-0498
practice_description: A security association lifetime of less than four hours (14400 seconds) is used for IPsec connections.

name: ISM-1000
practice_description: PFS is used for IPsec connections.

name: ISM-0628
practice_description: Gateways are implemented between networks belonging to different security domains.

name: ISM-0637
practice_description: Gateways implement a demilitarised zone if external parties require access to an organisation's services.

name: ISM-0631
practice_description: Gateways only allow explicitly authorised data flows.

name: ISM-1192
practice_description: Gateways inspect and filter data flows at the transport and above network layers.

name: ISM-1427
practice_description: Gateways perform ingress traffic filtering to detect and prevent IP source address spoofing.

name: ISM-1520
practice_description: System administrators for gateways undergo appropriate employment screening and, where necessary, hold an appropriate security clearance based on the sensitivity or classification of gateways.

name: ISM-0613
practice_description: System administrators for gateways that connect to Australian Eyes Only or Releasable To networks are Australian nationals.

name: ISM-1773
practice_description: System administrators for gateways that connect to Australian Government Access Only networks are Australian nationals or seconded foreign nationals.

name: ISM-0611
practice_description: System administrators for gateways are assigned the minimum privileges required to perform their duties.

name: ISM-0616
practice_description: Separation of duties is implemented in performing administrative activities for gateways.

name: ISM-0612
practice_description: System administrators for gateways are formally trained on the operation and management of gateways.

name: ISM-1774
practice_description: Gateways are managed via a secure path isolated from all connected networks.

name: ISM-0629
practice_description: For gateways between networks belonging to different security domains, any shared components are managed by system administrators for the higher security domain or by system administrators from a mutually-agreed third party.

capability_description: Authenticating to networks accessed via gateways
name: ISM-0619
practice_description: Users authenticate to other networks accessed via gateways.

capability_description: Authenticating to networks accessed via gateways
name: ISM-0622
practice_description: ICT equipment authenticates to other networks accessed via gateways.

name: ISM-1783
practice_description: Public IP addresses controlled by, or used by, an organisation are signed by valid ROA records.

name: ISM-0634
practice_description: The following events are logged for gateways: • data packets and data flows permitted through gateways • data packets and data flows attempting to leave gateways • real-time alerts for attempted intrusions.

name: ISM-1775
practice_description: Gateway event logs are stored centrally.

name: ISM-1037
practice_description: Gateways undergo testing following configuration changes, and at regular intervals no more than six months apart, to validate they conform to expected security configurations.

name: ISM-0100
practice_description: Gateways undergo a security assessment by an IRAP assessor at least every 24 months.

name: ISM-0626
practice_description: CDSs are implemented between SECRET or TOP SECRET networks and any other networks belonging to different security domains.

name: ISM-0597
practice_description: When planning, designing, implementing or introducing additional connectivity to CDSs, the ACSC is consulted and any directions provided by the ACSC are complied with.

name: ISM-0635
practice_description: CDSs implement isolated upward and downward network paths.

name: ISM-1522
practice_description: CDSs implement independent security-enforcing functions for upward and downward network paths.

name: ISM-1521
practice_description: CDSs implement protocol breaks at each network layer.

name: ISM-0670
practice_description: All security-relevant events generated by CDSs are logged.

name: ISM-1776
practice_description: CDS event logs are stored centrally.

name: ISM-1523
practice_description: A sample of security-relevant events relating to data transfer policies are taken at least every three months and assessed against security policies for CDSs to identify any operational failures.

name: ISM-0610
practice_description: Users are trained on the secure use of CDSs before access is granted.

name: ISM-1528
practice_description: Evaluated firewalls are used between an organisation's networks and public network infrastructure.

name: ISM-0639
practice_description: Evaluated firewalls are used between networks belonging to different security domains.

name: ISM-0643
practice_description: Evaluated diodes are used for controlling the data flow of unidirectional gateways between an organisation's networks and public network infrastructure.

name: ISM-0645
practice_description: Evaluated diodes used for controlling the data flow of unidirectional gateways between SECRET or TOP SECRET networks and public network infrastructure complete a high assurance evaluation.

name: ISM-1157
practice_description: Evaluated diodes are used for controlling the data flow of unidirectional gateways between networks.

name: ISM-1158
practice_description: Evaluated diodes used for controlling the data flow of unidirectional gateways between SECRET or TOP SECRET networks and any other networks complete a high assurance evaluation.

name: ISM-0258
practice_description: A web usage policy is developed, implemented and maintained.

name: ISM-0260
practice_description: All web access, including that by internal servers, is conducted through web proxies.

name: ISM-0261
practice_description: The following details are logged for websites accessed via web proxies: • address • date and time • user • amount of data uploaded and downloaded • internal and external IP addresses.

name: ISM-1777
practice_description: Web proxy event logs are stored centrally.

name: ISM-0963
practice_description: Web content filtering is implemented to filter potentially harmful web-based content.

name: ISM-0961
practice_description: Client-side active content is restricted by web content filters to an organisation-approved list of domain names.

name: ISM-1237
practice_description: Web content filtering is applied to outbound web traffic where appropriate.

name: ISM-0263
practice_description: TLS traffic communicated through gateways is decrypted and inspected.

capability_description: Allowing and blocking access to domain names
name: ISM-0958
practice_description: An organisation-approved list of domain names, or list of website categories, is implemented for all Hypertext Transfer Protocol and Hypertext Transfer Protocol Secure traffic communicated through gateways.

capability_description: Allowing and blocking access to domain names
name: ISM-1236
practice_description: Malicious domain names, dynamic domain names and domain names that can be registered anonymously for free are blocked by web content filters.

capability_description: Allowing and blocking access to domain names
name: ISM-1171
practice_description: Attempts to access websites through their IP addresses instead of their domain names are blocked by web content filters.

name: ISM-0659
practice_description: Files imported or exported via gateways or CDSs undergo content filtering checks.

name: ISM-0651
practice_description: Files identified by content filtering checks as malicious, or that cannot be inspected, are blocked.

name: ISM-0652
practice_description: Files identified by content filtering checks as suspicious are quarantined until reviewed and subsequently approved or not approved for release.

name: ISM-1524
practice_description: Content filters used by CDSs undergo rigorous security testing to ensure they perform as expected and cannot be bypassed.

name: ISM-1293
practice_description: Encrypted files imported or exported via gateways or CDSs are decrypted in order to undergo content filtering checks.

name: ISM-1289
practice_description: Archive files imported or exported via gateways or CDSs are unpacked in order to undergo content filtering checks.

name: ISM-1290
practice_description: Archive files are unpacked in a controlled manner to ensure content filter performance or availability is not adversely affected.

name: ISM-1288
practice_description: Files imported or exported via gateways or CDSs undergo antivirus scanning using multiple different scanning engines.

name: ISM-1389
practice_description: Executable files imported via gateways or CDSs are automatically executed in a sandbox to detect any suspicious behaviour.

name: ISM-0649
practice_description: Files imported or exported via gateways or CDSs are filtered for allowed file types.

name: ISM-1284
practice_description: Files imported or exported via gateways or CDSs undergo content validation.

name: ISM-1286
practice_description: Files imported or exported via gateways or CDSs undergo content conversion.

name: ISM-1287
practice_description: Files imported or exported via gateways or CDSs undergo content sanitisation.

name: ISM-0677
practice_description: Files imported or exported via gateways or CDSs that have a digital signature or cryptographic checksum are validated.

name: ISM-0591
practice_description: Evaluated peripheral switches are used when sharing peripherals between systems.

name: ISM-1457
practice_description: Evaluated peripheral switches used for sharing peripherals between SECRET and TOP SECRET systems, or between SECRET or TOP SECRET systems belonging to different security domains, preferably complete a high assurance evaluation.

name: ISM-1480
practice_description: Evaluated peripheral switches used for sharing peripherals between SECRET or TOP SECRET systems and any non-SECRET or TOP SECRET systems complete a high assurance evaluation.

name: ISM-0663
practice_description: Data transfer processes, and supporting data transfer procedures, are developed, implemented and maintained.

name: ISM-1535
practice_description: Processes, and supporting procedures, are developed, implemented and maintained to prevent AUSTEO, AGAO and REL data in both textual and non-textual formats from being exported to unsuitable foreign systems.

name: ISM-0661
practice_description: Users transferring data to and from systems are held accountable for data transfers they perform.

name: ISM-0657
practice_description: When manually importing data to systems, the data is scanned for malicious and active content.

name: ISM-1778
practice_description: When manually importing data to systems, all data that fails security checks is quarantined until reviewed and subsequently approved or not approved for release.

name: ISM-0664
practice_description: Data exported from SECRET and TOP SECRET systems is reviewed and authorised by a trusted source beforehand.

name: ISM-0675
practice_description: Data authorised for export from SECRET and TOP SECRET systems is digitally signed by a trusted source.

name: ISM-0665
practice_description: Trusted sources for SECRET and TOP SECRET systems are limited to people and services that have been authorised as such by an organisation's Chief Information Security Officer.

name: ISM-1187
practice_description: When manually exporting data from systems, the data is checked for unsuitable protective markings.

name: ISM-0669
practice_description: When manually exporting data from SECRET and TOP SECRET systems, digital signatures are validated and keyword checks are performed within all textual data.

name: ISM-1779
practice_description: When manually exporting data from systems, all data that fails security checks is quarantined until reviewed and subsequently approved or not approved for release.

name: ISM-1586
practice_description: Data transfer logs are used to record all data imports and exports from systems.

name: ISM-1294
practice_description: Data transfer logs for systems are partially verified at least monthly.

name: ISM-0660
practice_description: Data transfer logs for SECRET and TOP SECRET systems are fully verified at least monthly.
